_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:25:20Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ruby
ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "test"
require_relative "../config/environment"
require "rails/test_help"
require "ostruct"

# Minimal stub implementation for Minitest 6 (which removed Object#stub)
module StubHelper
  def stub_method(object, method_name, value_or_callable)
    original = object.method(method_name)

    replacement = if value_or_callable.respond_to?(:call)
      value_or_callable
    else
      ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { value_or_callable }
    end

    object.define_singleton_method(method_name) do |*args, **kwargs|
      replacement.call(*args, **kwargs)
    end

    yield
  ensure
    object.define_singleton_method(method_name, original)
  end
end

module WeatherTestHelper
  def build_mock_location(zip_code: "60601", latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298, address: "Chicago, Cook County, Illinois, 60601, United States")
    {
      zip_code: zip_code,
      latitude: latitude,
      longitude: longitude,
      formatted_address: address
    }
  end

  def build_mock_weather
    {
      current: CurrentWeather.new(
        temperature_f: 72.0,
        condition: "Partly Cloudy",
        icon: "⛅",
        humidity: 45,
        wind_mph: 8.5,
        feels_like_f: 70.0,
        weather_code: 2
      ),
      daily_forecast: [
        DayForecast.new(
          date: Date.today,
          high_f: 78.0,
          low_f: 62.0,
          condition: "Partly Cloudy",
          icon: "⛅",
          chance_of_rain: 10
        ),
        DayForecast.new(
          date: Date.today + 1,
          high_f: 75.0,
          low_f: 60.0,
          condition: "Mainly Clear",
          icon: "🌤️",
          chance_of_rain: 5
        ),
        DayForecast.new(
          date: Date.today + 2,
          high_f: 80.0,
          low_f: 65.0,
          condition: "Clear Sky",
          icon: "☀️",
          chance_of_rain: 0
        )
      ]
    }
  end

  def build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)
    weather = build_mock_weather
    ForecastResult.new(
      address: "Chicago, Cook County, Illinois, 60601, United States",
      zip_code: "60601",
      current: weather[:current],
      daily_forecast: weather[:daily_forecast],
      cached: cached,
      cached_at: Time.current
    )
  end

  def build_mock_open_meteo_response
    {
      "current" => {
        "temperature_2m" => 72.0,
        "relative_humidity_2m" => 45,
        "apparent_temperature" => 70.0,
        "weather_code" => 2,
        "wind_speed_10m" => 8.5
      },
      "daily" => {
        "time" => [ Date.today.to_s, (Date.today + 1).to_s, (Date.today + 2).to_s ],
        "weather_code" => [ 2, 1, 0 ],
        "temperature_2m_max" => [ 78.0, 75.0, 80.0 ],
        "temperature_2m_min" => [ 62.0, 60.0, 65.0 ],
        "precipitation_probability_max" => [ 10, 5, 0 ]
      }
    }
  end

  def stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: "60601", coordinates: [ 41.8781, -87.6298 ], address: "Chicago, Cook County, Illinois, 60601, United States")
    mock = OpenStruct.new(
      postal_code: postal_code,
      coordinates: coordinates,
      address: address
    )
    [ mock ]
  end

  def mock_http_response(code, body)
    response = OpenStruct.new(
      code: code,
      success?: code == 200,
      parsed_response: body
    )
    response.define_singleton_method(:dig) { |*keys| body.dig(*keys) }
    response.define_singleton_method(:[]) { |key| body[key] }
    response
  end
end

module ActiveSupport
  class TestCase
    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)
    include WeatherTestHelper
    include StubHelper
  end
end

```

```ruby
require "test_helper"

class GeocodingServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
  test "returns location data for a valid address" do
    results = stub_geocoder_result
    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
      location = GeocodingService.call("Chicago, IL")
      assert_equal "60601", location[:zip_code]
      assert_in_delta 41.8781, location[:latitude], 0.001
      assert_in_delta(-87.6298, location[:longitude], 0.001)
      assert_includes location[:formatted_address], "Chicago"
    end
  end

  test "raises GeocodingError for unknown address" do
    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { [] }) do
      error = assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
        GeocodingService.call("xyznonexistent")
      end
      assert_includes error.message, "Could not find location"
    end
  end

  test "raises GeocodingError when postal code is missing" do
    results = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: nil)
    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
      error = assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
        GeocodingService.call("Somewhere without zip")
      end
      assert_includes error.message, "Could not determine zip code"
    end
  end

  test "raises GeocodingError when postal code is blank" do
    results = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: "")
    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
      assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
        GeocodingService.call("Blank zip address")
      end
    end
  end
end

```ruby
require "test_helper"

class WeatherServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
  test "parses successful API response into structured weather data" do
    response = mock_http_response(200, build_mock_open_meteo_response)

    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
      result = WeatherService.call(latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298)

      assert_instance_of CurrentWeather, result[:current]
      assert_equal 72.0, result[:current].temperature_f
      assert_equal "Partly Cloudy", result[:current].condition
      assert_equal 45, result[:current].humidity
      assert_equal 8.5, result[:current].wind_mph
      assert_equal 70.0, result[:current].feels_like_f
      assert_equal 2, result[:current].weather_code

      assert_equal 3, result[:daily_forecast].length
      first_day = result[:daily_forecast].first
      assert_instance_of DayForecast, first_day
      assert_equal 78.0, first_day.high_f
      assert_equal 62.0, first_day.low_f
      assert_equal 10, first_day.chance_of_rain
    end
  end

  test "raises WeatherApiError on non-success response" do
    response = mock_http_response(400, { "reason" => "Bad request" })

    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
      error = assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
        WeatherService.call(latitude: 0, longitude: 0)
      end
      assert_includes error.message, "Bad request"
    end
  end

  test "handles nil daily data gracefully" do
    response_data = build_mock_open_meteo_response
    response_data["daily"] = nil
    response = mock_http_response(200, response_data)

    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
      result = WeatherService.call(latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298)
      assert_equal [], result[:daily_forecast]
    end
  end

  test "maps WMO weather codes to conditions and emoji" do
    response_data = build_mock_open_meteo_response
    response_data["current"]["weather_code"] = 95
    response = mock_http_response(200, response_data)

    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
      result = WeatherService.call(latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298)
      assert_equal "Thunderstorm", result[:current].condition
      assert_equal 95, result[:current].weather_code
    end
  end
end

```

```ruby
require "test_helper"

class ForecastServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
  setup do
    Rails.cache.clear
  end

  test "returns fresh forecast on first request" do
    location = build_mock_location
    weather = build_mock_weather

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kwargs) { weather }) do
        result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")

        assert_instance_of ForecastResult, result
        assert_equal "60601", result.zip_code
        assert_not result.cached?
        assert_not_nil result.cached_at
        assert_equal 72.0, result.current.temperature_f
        assert_equal 3, result.daily_forecast.length
      end
    end
  end

  test "returns cached forecast on second request for same zip code" do
    location = build_mock_location
    weather = build_mock_weather
    call_count = 0

    weather_stub = ->(**_kwargs) {
      call_count += 1
      weather
    }

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, weather_stub) do
        first_result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert_not first_result.cached?

        second_result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert second_result.cached?
        assert_equal 1, call_count, "WeatherService should only be called once"
      end
    end
  end

  test "different addresses with same zip code share cache" do
    location = build_mock_location
    weather = build_mock_weather
    call_count = 0

    weather_stub = ->(**_kwargs) {
      call_count += 1
      weather
    }

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, weather_stub) do
        ForecastService.call("123 Main St, Chicago, IL")
        result = ForecastService.call("456 Oak Ave, Chicago, IL")
        assert result.cached?
        assert_equal 1, call_count
      end
    end
  end

  test "cache expires after 30 minutes" do
    location = build_mock_location
    weather = build_mock_weather

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kwargs) { weather }) do
        first_result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert_not first_result.cached?

        travel 31.minutes

        second_result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert_not second_result.cached?, "Should be fresh after cache expiry"
      end
    end
  end

  test "propagates GeocodingError" do
    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise GeocodingService::GeocodingError, "Not found" }) do
      assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
        ForecastService.call("invalid")
      end
    end
  end

  test "propagates WeatherApiError" do
    location = build_mock_location

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kwargs) { raise WeatherService::WeatherApiError, "API down" }) do
        assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
          ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        end
      end
    end
  end

  test "cached_at timestamp is set on both fresh and cached results" do
    location = build_mock_location
    weather = build_mock_weather

    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kwargs) { weather }) do
        fresh = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert_not_nil fresh.cached_at

        cached = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
        assert_not_nil cached.cached_at
      end
    end
  end
end

```

require "test_helper"

class ForecastsControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  test "index renders the search form" do
    get root_path
    assert_response :success
    assert_select "input[name='address']"
    assert_select "input[type='submit']"
  end

  test "show renders forecast for valid address" do
    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_response :success
      assert_match(/72/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Just updated/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "show renders cached indicator for cached result" do
    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: true)

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_response :success
      assert_match(/Cached result from/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "show redirects with alert for blank address" do
    get forecast_path, params: { address: "" }
    assert_redirected_to root_path
    follow_redirect!
    assert_match(/Please enter an address/, response.body)
  end

  test "show redirects with alert for missing address" do
    get forecast_path
    assert_redirected_to root_path
    follow_redirect!
    assert_match(/Please enter an address/, response.body)
  end

  test "show redirects with alert on geocoding error" do
    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise GeocodingService::GeocodingError, "Could not find location" }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "xyznonexistent" }
      assert_redirected_to root_path
      follow_redirect!
      assert_match(/Could not find location/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "show redirects with alert on weather API error" do
    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise WeatherService::WeatherApiError, "Service unavailable" }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_redirected_to root_path
      follow_redirect!
      assert_match(/Weather data unavailable/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "show displays forecast details including high low and extended forecast" do
    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_response :success
      assert_match(/72/, response.body)
      assert_match(/78/, response.body)
      assert_match(/62/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Today/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Humidity/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Wind/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Feels Like/, response.body)
    end
  end
end

```ruby
require "test_helper"

class ForecastFlowTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    Rails.cache.clear
  end

  test "full flow: search form to forecast display" do
    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
      get root_path
      assert_response :success
      assert_select "h1", /Weather/
      assert_select "input[name='address']"

      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_response :success

      assert_match(/72/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Chicago/, response.body)
      assert_match(/60601/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Just updated/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Feels Like/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Humidity/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Wind/, response.body)
      assert_match(/5-Day Forecast/, response.body)
      assert_match(/Today/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "cached result shows cache indicator" do
    fresh_forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)
    cached_forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: true)
    call_count = 0

    service_stub = ->(_addr) {
      call_count += 1
      call_count == 1 ? fresh_forecast : cached_forecast
    }

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, service_stub) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_match(/Just updated/, response.body)

      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_match(/Cached result from/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "error flow shows flash and returns to search" do
    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise GeocodingService::GeocodingError, "Could not find location for 'invalid'" }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "invalid" }
      assert_redirected_to root_path
      follow_redirect!
      assert_response :success
      assert_match(/Could not find location/, response.body)
    end
  end

  test "search form is present on forecast results page" do
    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)

    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
      assert_select "input[name='address']"
      assert_select "input[type='submit']"
    end
  end

  test "blank address redirects back with error" do
    get forecast_path, params: { address: "" }
    assert_redirected_to root_path
    follow_redirect!
    assert_match(/Please enter an address/, response.body)
  end
end

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:26:37Z)**_

Found it - `config.cache_store = :null_store` in test.rb means all cache reads return nil. I need to use `:memory_store` for tests so the caching logic actually works.

Review the Rails app at /Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/ for requirements compliance and configuration correctness:

**Requirements checklist:**
1. Ruby on Rails? Check Gemfile and config.
2. Accepts address as input? Check the form and controller.
3. Retrieves forecast data including current temperature? Check WeatherService.
4. Retrieves high/low? Check the show view and models.
5. Extended forecast? Check daily_forecast in show view.
6. Displays forecast details to user? Check show.html.erb.
7. Caches for 30 minutes by zip code? Check ForecastService cache logic.
8. Shows cache indicator? Check show.html.erb cache indicator section.

**Configuration review:**
- Gemfile - are all gems needed? Any missing?
- config/environments/development.rb - cache configuration
- config/environments/test.rb - test cache config
- config/routes.rb - are routes correct?
- config/initializers/geocoder.rb - is it configured properly?
- Procfile.dev - will `bin/dev` work?

**Potential gaps:**
- What happens if the Open-Meteo API is down?
- What happens with non-US addresses that have no zip code?
- What about rate limiting on Nominatim?
- Is the cache key safe from injection?
- Is the API URL hardcoded correctly?

Read ALL config files and report specific issues.

1	class ForecastsController < ApplicationController
2	  def index
3	  end
4	
5	  def show
6	    address = params[:address]
7	    if address.blank?
8	      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Please enter an address."
9	      return
10	    end
11	
12	    @forecast = ForecastService.call(address)
13	    @address_query = address
14	  rescue GeocodingService::GeocodingError => e
15	    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
16	  rescue WeatherService::WeatherApiError => e
17	    redirect_to root_path, alert: "Weather data unavailable: #{e.message}"
18	  end
19	end
20
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 00:31:36Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/forecast_service.rb`

```text
1	class ForecastService
2	  CACHE_EXPIRY = 30.minutes
3	  CACHE_PREFIX = "forecast_v1"
4	
5	  def self.call(address)
6	    new(address).call
7	  end
8	
9	  def initialize(address)
10	    @address = address
11	  end
12	
13	  def call
14	    location = GeocodingService.call(@address)
15	    zip_code = location[:zip_code]
16	    cache_key = "#{CACHE_PREFIX}/#{zip_code}"
17	
18	    cached_data = Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
19	
20	    if cached_data
21	      build_result(location, cached_data[:weather], zip_code, cached: true, cached_at: cached_data[:fetched_at])
22	    else
23	      weather = WeatherService.call(latitude: location[:latitude], longitude: location[:longitude])
24	      fetched_at = Time.current
25	      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, { weather: weather, fetched_at: fetched_at }, expires_in: CACHE_EXPIRY)
26	      build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached: false, cached_at: fetched_at)
27	    end
28	  end
29	
30	  private
31	
32	  def build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached:, cached_at:)
33	    ForecastResult.new(
34	      address: location[:formatted_address],
35	      zip_code: zip_code,
36	      current: weather[:current],
37	      daily_forecast: weather[:daily_forecast],
38	      cached: cached,
39	      cached_at: cached_at
40	    )
41	  end
42	end
43
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 00:31:36Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/weather_service.rb`

```text
1	class WeatherService
2	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
3	
4	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
5	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
6	
7	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
8	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
9	  end
10	
11	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
12	    @latitude = latitude
13	    @longitude = longitude
14	  end
15	
16	  def call
17	    response = fetch_forecast
18	    validate_response!(response)
19	    parse_response(response)
20	  end
21	
22	  private
23	
24	  def fetch_forecast
25	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, query: {
26	      latitude: @latitude,
27	      longitude: @longitude,
28	      current: "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m",
29	      daily: "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max",
30	      temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
31	      wind_speed_unit: "mph",
32	      forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
33	      timezone: "auto"
34	    })
35	  end
36	
37	  def validate_response!(response)
38	    return if response.success?
39	
40	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Unknown API error (HTTP #{response.code})"
41	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
42	  end
43	
44	  def parse_response(response)
45	    {
46	      current: build_current_weather(response["current"]),
47	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
48	    }
49	  end
50	
51	  def build_current_weather(data)
52	    return nil if data.nil?
53	
54	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
55	    CurrentWeather.new(
56	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
57	      condition: WeatherCode.description(code),
58	      icon: WeatherCode.emoji(code),
59	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
60	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
61	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
62	      weather_code: code
63	    )
64	  end
65	
66	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
67	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
68	
69	    data["time"].each_with_index.map do |date_str, i|
70	      code = data["weather_code"][i].to_i
71	      DayForecast.new(
72	        date: Date.parse(date_str),
73	        high_f: data["temperature_2m_max"][i],
74	        low_f: data["temperature_2m_min"][i],
75	        condition: WeatherCode.description(code),
76	        icon: WeatherCode.emoji(code),
77	        chance_of_rain: data["precipitation_probability_max"][i] || 0
78	      )
79	    end

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/geocoding_service.rb`

```text
1	class GeocodingService
2	  class GeocodingError < StandardError; end
3	
4	  def self.call(address)
5	    new(address).call
6	  end
7	
8	  def initialize(address)
9	    @address = address
10	  end
11	
12	  def call
13	    results = Geocoder.search(@address)
14	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not find location for '#{@address}'" if results.empty?
15	
16	    result = results.first
17	    zip_code = extract_zip_code(result)
18	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not determine zip code for '#{@address}'. Try entering a more specific address with a zip code." if zip_code.blank?
19	
20	    {
21	      zip_code: zip_code,
22	      latitude: result.coordinates[0],
23	      longitude: result.coordinates[1],
24	      formatted_address: result.address
25	    }
26	  end
27	
28	  private
29	
30	  def extract_zip_code(result)
31	    result.postal_code.presence
32	  end
33	end
34
```
</details></tool-use>

38	
39	    <%# Hero: Location + Temperature %>
40	    <div class="text-center mb-8">

42	        <%= @forecast.address %>
43	      </h1>

45

67	        <div class="text-center">
68	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Feels Like</p>
69	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><%= format_temperature(@forecast.current.feels_like_f) %>&deg;</p>

78	        </div>

91	            <%# Day name %>
92	            <div class="w-20">
93	              <p class="text-white font-medium text-sm">
94	                <%= index == 0 ? "Today" : day.date.strftime("%A") %>

102	                  <path d="M12 2.69l5.66 5.66a8 8 0 1 1-11.31 0z"/>
103	                </svg>

111	            </div>
112	
113	            <%# Temps %>

117	            </div>
118	          </div>
119	        <% end %>
120	      </div>
121	    <% end %>
122

125	      Powered by Open-Meteo &amp; OpenStreetMap

127	  </div>

12	    61 => { description: "Slight Rain",      emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-600 via-slate-600 to-gray-600" },

15	    71 => { description: "Slight Snow",      emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-200 via-slate-300 to-gray-400" },
16	    73 => { description: "Moderate Snow",    emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
17	    75 => { description: "Heavy Snow",       emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
18	    77 => { description: "Snow Grains",      emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
19	    80 => { description: "Slight Showers",   emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-600" },
20	    81 => { description: "Moderate Showers", emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
21	    82 => { description: "Violent Showers",  emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
22	    85 => { description: "Slight Snow Showers", emoji: "🌨️", gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
23	    86 => { description: "Heavy Snow Showers",  emoji: "❄️",  gradient: "from-slate-400 via-gray-500 to-gray-600" },
24	    95 => { description: "Thunderstorm",     emoji: "⛈️",   gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
25	    96 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Slight Hail", emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
26	    99 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Heavy Hail",  emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-800 via-slate-900 to-gray-950" }
27	  }.freeze
28	
29	  DEFAULT = { description: "Unknown", emoji: "🌡️", gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-blue-600" }.freeze
30	
31	  def self.for(code)
32	    CODES.fetch(code, DEFAULT)
33	  end
34	
35	  def self.description(code)
36	    self.for(code)[:description]
37	  end
38	
39	  def self.emoji(code)
40	    self.for(code)[:emoji]
41	  end
42

45	  end
46	end

require "active_support/core_ext/integer/time"

Rails.application.configure do
  # Settings specified here will take precedence over those in config/application.rb.

  # Code is not reloaded between requests.
  config.enable_reloading = false

  # Eager load code on boot for better performance and memory savings (ignored by Rake tasks).
  config.eager_load = true

  # Full error reports are disabled.
  config.consider_all_requests_local = false

  # Turn on fragment caching in view templates.
  config.action_controller.perform_caching = true

  # Cache assets for far-future expiry since they are all digest stamped.
  config.public_file_server.headers = { "cache-control" => "public, max-age=#{1.year.to_i}" }

  # Enable serving of images, stylesheets, and JavaScripts from an asset server.
  # config.asset_host = "http://assets.example.com"

  # Assume all access to the app is happening through a SSL-terminating reverse proxy.
  config.assume_ssl = true

  # Force all access to the app over SSL, use Strict-Transport-Security, and use secure cookies.
  config.force_ssl = true

  # Skip http-to-https redirect for the default health check endpoint.
  # config.ssl_options = { redirect: { exclude: ->(request) { request.path == "/up" } } }

  # Log to STDOUT with the current request id as a default log tag.
  config.log_tags = [ :request_id ]
  config.logger   = ActiveSupport::TaggedLogging.logger(STDOUT)

  # Change to "debug" to log everything (including potentially personally-identifiable information!).
  config.log_level = ENV.fetch("RAILS_LOG_LEVEL", "info")

  # Prevent health checks from clogging up the logs.
  config.silence_healthcheck_path = "/up"

  # Don't log any deprecations.
  config.active_support.report_deprecations = false

  # Replace the default in-process memory cache store with a durable alternative.
  # config.cache_store = :mem_cache_store

  # Enable locale fallbacks for I18n (makes lookups for any locale fall back to

#!/usr/bin/env sh

if ! gem list foreman -i --silent; then
  echo "Installing foreman..."
  gem install foreman
fi

# Default to port 3000 if not specified
export PORT="${PORT:-3000}"

# Let the debug gem allow remote connections,
# but avoid loading until `debugger` is called
export RUBY_DEBUG_OPEN="true"
export RUBY_DEBUG_LAZY="true"

exec foreman start -f Procfile.dev "$@"
```

### CONFIGURATION REVIEW

#### Gemfile (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/Gemfile`)
- Status: MOSTLY GOOD
- **Present and correct:**
  - `rails ~> 8.1.2`

#### config/routes.rb (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/routes.rb`)
- Status: CORRECT

- `GET /forecast` → forecasts#show
- Health check endpoint present

#### config/initializers/geocoder.rb (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/initializers/geocoder.rb`)
- Status: EXCELLENT
- Uses Nominatim (OpenStreetMap)
- HTTPS enabled
- User-Agent set appropriately
- 5-second timeout
- Cache configured with 1-day expiration
- Cache prefix "geocoder:" avoids collisions

#### config/environments/development.rb (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/environments/development.rb`)
- Status: CORRECT
- Cache store: `:memory_store`
- Caching controlled by `tmp/caching-dev.txt` file
- Proper for development (run `rails dev:cache` to toggle)

#### config/environments/test.rb (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/environments/test.rb`)
- Status: CORRECT
- Cache store: `:memory_store`
- Clean configuration for tests

#### config/environments/production.rb
- Status: CRITICAL ISSUE FOUND

- **This means production falls back to nil_store (no caching)**

- Status: CORRECT

---

### POTENTIAL GAPS AND SECURITY ISSUES

#### Critical Issues

1. **Production Cache Store NOT Configured**
   - Location: `config/environments/production.rb`
   - The line `config.cache_store = :mem_cache_store` is commented out
   - In production, this falls back to `:null_store` (no caching)
   - 30-minute cache logic is completely bypassed
   - **Fix:** Configure actual cache store (Redis, Memcached, or similar)

#### High-Risk Issues

2. **API Down Scenario - Incomplete Error Handling**
   - Open-Meteo API failures: `WeatherApiError` caught and displayed
   - Location: `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/controllers/forecasts_controller.rb` lines 16-17
   - **Issue:** Generic message shown to user; no retry logic or fallback

3. **Non-US Addresses with No Zip Code**
   - GeocodingService explicitly rejects addresses without postal_code
   - Location: `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/services/geocoding_service.rb` line 18
   - **Issue:** International users get error "Could not determine zip code for '...'. Try entering a more specific address with a zip code."
   - **Consequence:** App only works for addresses with postal codes (mostly US, some others)

4. **Nominatim Rate Limiting Not Addressed**
   - No rate limit headers being checked or respected
   - Location: `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/services/weather_service.rb` and `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/services/geocoding_service.rb`
   - Nominatim ToS requires rate limiting
   - **Issue:** No exponential backoff or request throttling
   - **Recommendation:** Implement rate limit handling

#### Medium-Risk Issues

5. **Cache Key Injection Risk**
   - Cache key: `"#{CACHE_PREFIX}/#{zip_code}"`
   - Zip code comes from Nominatim geocoder results
   - **Risk Level:** LOW (Nominatim validates/sanitizes)
   - **However:** If Nominatim returns unusual characters, could theoretically cause issues

6. **Open-Meteo API URL Hardcoded**
   - Base URL: `"https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"`
   - Location: `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/services/weather_service.rb` line 4
   - **Issue:** No way to switch to staging/mirror endpoint
   - **Recommendation:** Make configurable via ENV var

7. **No Validation of Coordinates**
   - Latitude/longitude from Nominatim used directly in weather API call
   - No validation that they're within expected ranges
   - **Risk:** Nominatim bugs could send invalid coordinates

8. **Timeout Too Short for Nominatim**
   - Geocoder timeout: 5 seconds
   - Nominatim can be slower in high-traffic periods
   - **Recommendation:** Increase to 10 seconds

#### Low-Risk Issues

9. **No Caching for Geocoding Results**
   - Wait, actually this IS configured (geocoder.rb line 9-13)
   - Geocoder results cached 1 day in Rails.cache
   - This is good

10. **HTTParty Response Validation**
   - Only checks `response.success?`
   - No timeout handling for HTTParty calls
   - Open-Meteo might return 200 with error message
   - Location: `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/services/weather_service.rb` line 37-42

---

### DETAILED FINDINGS SUMMARY

**Critical (Fix immediately):**
- Production cache store not configured - no actual caching happens

**High Priority (Should fix before production):**
- No error recovery for API failures (silent failures possible)
- International address support broken (requires postal code)
- Nominatim rate limiting not handled

**Recommended Improvements:**
- Make Open-Meteo URL configurable
- Increase Nominatim timeout to 10 seconds
- Add retry logic with exponential backoff
- Validate latitude/longitude ranges
- Add request rate limiting

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested building a fully functional Ruby on Rails weather forecast application from scratch. Specific requirements:
   - Accept an address as input
   - Retrieve forecast data: current temperature, high/low, extended forecast
   - Display forecast details to user
   - Cache forecast by zip code for 30 minutes, with cache indicator when results come from cache
   - Apple-style design (glass morphism, SF Pro fonts, translucent cards, weather-contextual gradients)
   - All real data — no mock/placeholder data visible to users
   - All unit tests and end-to-end tests in place and passing
   - Best coding practices: readability, well-commented, maintainable, optimized for performance
   - No bugs, errors, gaps
   - The final request was to review the entire project end-to-end to ensure it will work and run without any error, issue, bug, or gap.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Ruby on Rails 8.1.2 with Ruby 3.4.5
   - Tailwind CSS v4.2.2 via tailwindcss-rails 4.4.0 (standalone binary, no Node.js)
   - Open-Meteo API for weather data (free, no API key)
   - Nominatim/OpenStreetMap for geocoding via geocoder gem (free, no API key)
   - WMO (World Meteorological Organization) weather codes mapped to descriptions, emoji, and Tailwind gradient classes
   - Rails.cache with :memory_store for 30-minute TTL caching by zip code
   - Service object pattern (GeocodingService, WeatherService, ForecastService orchestrator)
   - PORO (Plain Old Ruby Object) value objects (ForecastResult, CurrentWeather, DayForecast, WeatherCode)
   - No database (--skip-active-record) — purely service-based app
   - Apple Weather-inspired design: glass morphism (bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl), system font stack, thin/light font weights, responsive sizing
   - Minitest 6.0.3 (shipped with Ruby 3.4.5) — removed Object#stub, required custom StubHelper
   - Hotwire/Turbo Drive for page navigation
   - Frozen string literals throughout
   - Accessibility: ARIA labels, semantic HTML, form labels

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **Gemfile** (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/Gemfile`)
     - Core app gems plus geocoder ~> 1.8 and httparty ~> 0.24
     - Was corrupted with duplicate lines 54-56, fixed by removing duplicates
     ```ruby
     gem "geocoder", "~> 1.8"
     gem "httparty", "~> 0.24"
     ```

   - **config/routes.rb** (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/routes.rb`)
     - Two routes: root for search form, GET /forecast for results
     ```ruby
     root "forecasts#index"
     get "forecast", to: "forecasts#show", as: :forecast
     ```

   - **config/initializers/geocoder.rb** (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/initializers/geocoder.rb`)
     - Nominatim backend, User-Agent header, 10s timeout, 1-day geocoder result caching
     ```ruby
     Geocoder.configure(
       lookup: :nominatim,
       language: :en,
       use_https: true,
       http_headers: { "User-Agent" => "ForecastDemo/1.0 (Rails weather forecast demo app)" },
       timeout: 10,
       cache: Rails.cache,
       cache_options: { expiration: 1.day, prefix: "geocoder:" }
     )
     ```

   - **app/models/weather_code.rb** (`/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/app/models/weather_code.rb`)
     - Static WMO code mapping to {description, emoji, gradient} with deep-frozen CODES hash
     - 25 weather codes mapped, DEFAULT for unknown codes
     - Class methods: `.for(code)`, `.description(code)`, `.emoji(code)`, `.gradient(code)`
     ```ruby
     CODES = {
       0  => { description: "Clear Sky", emoji: "☀️", gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
       # ... 24 more entries
     }.each_value(&:freeze).freeze
     ```

   - **app/models/current_weather.rb** — Value object with: temperature_f, condition, icon, humidity, wind_mph, feels_like_f, weather_code

   - **app/models/day_forecast.rb** — Value object with: date, high_f, low_f, condition, icon, chance_of_rain

   - **app/models/forecast_result.rb** — Wraps complete forecast with cache metadata. Defaults daily_forecast to [] if nil. Has `cached?` predicate.
     ```ruby
     def initialize(address:, zip_code:, current:, daily_forecast:, cached: false, cached_at: nil)
       @daily_forecast = daily_forecast || []
     end
     ```

   - **app/services/geocoding_service.rb** — Converts address to {zip_code, latitude, longitude, formatted_address}. Strips whitespace, truncates long addresses in error messages. Raises GeocodingError.
     ```ruby
     def initialize(address)
       @address = address.to_s.strip
     end
     ```

   - **app/services/weather_service.rb** — Calls Open-Meteo API with HTTParty (10s timeout). Extracts CURRENT_PARAMS and DAILY_PARAMS to constants. Optimized: fetches WeatherCode entry once per iteration. Safe Date.parse with rescue.
     ```ruby
     CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
     DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
     
     def parse_date(date_str)
       Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
     rescue Date::Error
       Date.current
     end
     ```

   - **app/services/forecast_service.rb** — Orchestrator with 30-min cache by zip code. Uses Rails.cache.read/write (not .fetch) to track cache hit/miss. Cache key: "forecast_v1/{zip_code}".
     ```ruby
     CACHE_EXPIRY = 30.minutes
     CACHE_PREFIX = "forecast_v1"
     ```

   - **app/controllers/forecasts_controller.rb** — index (form) + show (results). Rescues GeocodingError and WeatherApiError, redirects with flash alerts.

   - **app/helpers/forecasts_helper.rb** — `gradient_class_for(weather_code)` and `format_temperature(temp)` (nil-safe, returns "--").

   - **app/views/layouts/application.html.erb** — `<html lang="en">`, `<main>` wrapper, Apple system font stack in inline style, meta description tag.

   - **app/views/forecasts/index.html.erb** — Full-screen blue gradient, glass card search form, flash alerts with role="alert", sr-only labels, aria-hidden SVG icons.

   - **app/views/forecasts/show.html.erb** — Weather-contextual gradient background, glass morphism cards. Sections: search bar, cache indicator (amber cached/green fresh with role="status"), hero temp (responsive text-7xl sm:text-8xl), weather details grid, 5-day forecast list. Nil-safe: `@forecast.cached_at&.strftime("%-I:%M %p") || "recently"`. ARIA labels on sections and emoji spans.

   - **app/assets/tailwind/application.css** — Tailwind v4 CSS-based config with Apple font theme.
     ```css
     @import "tailwindcss";
     @theme {
       --font-sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "SF Pro Display", "SF Pro Text", "Helvetica Neue", "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif;
     }
     ```

   - **config/environments/test.rb** — Changed `cache_store` from `:null_store` to `:memory_store` so caching tests work.

   - **config/environments/production.rb** — Added explicit `config.cache_store = :memory_store` (was commented out).

   - **test/test_helper.rb** — Custom StubHelper module (defines `stub_method` using `define_singleton_method`/ensure), WeatherTestHelper with factory methods (build_mock_location, build_mock_weather, build_mock_forecast_result, build_mock_open_meteo_response, stub_geocoder_result, mock_http_response). Parallelization set to workers: 1 for thread safety.

   - **Test files (7 files, 52 tests, 291 assertions):**
     - test/services/geocoding_service_test.rb (6 tests)
     - test/services/weather_service_test.rb (8 tests)
     - test/services/forecast_service_test.rb (9 tests)
     - test/controllers/forecasts_controller_test.rb (11 tests including XSS test)
     - test/models/forecast_result_test.rb (4 tests)
     - test/models/weather_code_test.rb (7 tests)
     - test/integration/forecast_flow_test.rb (7 tests)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **Unicode surrogate pairs in Ruby:** Used `\uD83C\uDF24\uFE0F` (JavaScript surrogate pair syntax) which is invalid in Ruby 3.4. Fixed by using literal emoji characters (☀️, ⛅, 🌧️, etc.) in both weather_code.rb and test_helper.rb.
   
   - **Minitest 6 removed Object#stub and minitest/mock:** `require "minitest/mock"` raised LoadError. Fixed by creating a custom `StubHelper` module with `stub_method(object, method_name, value_or_callable)` that uses `define_singleton_method` and restores original method in ensure block.
   
   - **OpenStruct not auto-loaded in Ruby 3.4.5:** `NameError: uninitialized constant OpenStruct`. Fixed by adding `require "ostruct"` to test_helper.rb. Note: this produces a deprecation warning about Ruby 3.5 (ostruct leaving default gems). Attempted to add `gem "ostruct"` to Gemfile but disk space issues prevented bundle install; reverted since it's just a warning.
   
   - **Test cache store was :null_store:** Cache tests for ForecastService failed because test.rb used `config.cache_store = :null_store` (default Rails). Fixed by changing to `:memory_store`.
   
   - **Gemfile corruption:** Lines 54-56 had garbled duplicate content (`ithub.com/rails/web-console]` etc.). Fixed by removing the duplicate lines.
   
   - **Sandbox disk space exhaustion:** `/private/tmp/claude-502/` ran out of space, preventing bash commands. Worked around by reverting unnecessary changes (ostruct gem).
   
   - **User feedback - "No signup needed":** User didn't want to create a WeatherAPI.com account. Switched entire architecture from WeatherAPI.com to Open-Meteo API (free, no key). Required different API response parsing, WMO weather codes instead of text descriptions, emoji icons instead of API-provided icon URLs.
   
   - **User feedback - "Apple style" and "real data":** User explicitly required Apple Weather-inspired design and no mock data visible to end users. Added glass morphism, SF Pro font stack, weather-contextual gradients, responsive typography.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved the "no API key" constraint by switching to Open-Meteo (free) + Nominatim (free)
   - Solved Minitest 6 stubbing by implementing custom StubHelper
   - Solved Ruby 3.4 breaking changes (OpenStruct, frozen string implications)
   - Solved Tailwind v4 config (CSS-based @theme instead of tailwind.config.js)
   - Solved cache testing by configuring memory_store in test environment
   - Solved accessibility gaps identified in review (labels, ARIA, semantic HTML)
   - Solved nil safety for cached_at and daily_forecast
   - Solved performance: optimized WeatherCode lookups, added HTTP timeouts
   
   **Remaining issues identified during final review (not yet fixed):**
   - Tailwind CSS may not scan .rb files — gradient classes in weather_code.rb might not be compiled. Need `@source` directive in application.css.
   - show.html.erb line 1 calls `@forecast.current.weather_code` — if current is nil (unlikely but possible if API returns no current data), this crashes with NoMethodError. Need validation in WeatherService or nil guard.
   - `allow_browser versions: :modern` in ApplicationController could block older browsers.

6. All User Messages:
   - **Message 1:** "Design and Build a fully functional app: Requirements: Must be done in Ruby on Rails, Accept an address as input, Retrieve forecast data for the given address. This should include, at minimum, the current temperature. Retrieve high/low and/or extended forecast. Display the requested forecast details to the user. Cache the forecast details for 30 minutes for all subsequent requests by zip codes. Display indicator if result is pulled from cache. Use agent team. Use any resource if needed. Deep thinking. 8. Current Work:
   I was performing a systematic end-to-end review of the entire project per the user's request: "review the entire project make sure it will work and run with no error, issue, bug, gap, etc.. let's think step by step."
   
   I had read ALL application files systematically:
   - Gemfile, routes, geocoder initializer, environment configs (dev, test, production)
   - All 4 models (weather_code.rb, current_weather.rb, day_forecast.rb, forecast_result.rb)
   - All 3 services (geocoding_service.rb, weather_service.rb, forecast_service.rb)
   - Controller, helper
   - All 3 views (layout, index, show)
   - Tailwind CSS config, Procfile.dev, application_controller.rb, content_security_policy.rb
   
   I had identified issues during analysis but had NOT yet applied any fixes when the summary was triggered. The identified issues are:
   
   1. **Tailwind source scanning** — `app/assets/tailwind/application.css` has `@import "tailwindcss"` but no `@source` directive. Tailwind v4 auto-detects sources but may not scan `.rb` files. The gradient classes defined in `app/models/weather_code.rb` (e.g., `"from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500"`) may not be included in the compiled CSS output, causing the show page to have no gradient background.
   
   2. **Nil safety for @forecast.current** — `show.html.erb` line 1: `<%= gradient_class_for(@forecast.current.weather_code) %>` will crash with NoMethodError if `@forecast.current` is nil. WeatherService#build_current_weather returns nil when data is nil, and this propagates through ForecastService into the view.
   
   3. **allow_browser versions: :modern** in ApplicationController could block older browsers.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Fix the two critical issues identified during the final review, then run the dev server to verify:
   
   1. Add Tailwind `@source` directive in `app/assets/tailwind/application.css` to ensure `.rb` files are scanned for gradient classes
   2. Add validation in `WeatherService#parse_response` to raise `WeatherApiError` if current weather data is nil, preventing view crashes
   3. Run `bin/rails test` to verify all 52 tests still pass
   4. Start `bin/dev` and test with real addresses (Chicago IL, New York NY, invalid address) to verify the app works end-to-end

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/weather_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Fetches weather data from the Open-Meteo API (https://open-meteo.com).
4	# No API key required. Returns current conditions and a multi-day forecast.
5	#
6	# Open-Meteo uses WMO weather interpretation codes (mapped by WeatherCode).
7	# API docs: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs
8	class WeatherService
9	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
12	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
13	
14	  # Current weather fields requested from Open-Meteo
15	  CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
16	
17	  # Daily forecast fields requested from Open-Meteo
18	  DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
19	
20	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
21	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
22	  end
23	
24	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
25	    @latitude = latitude
26	    @longitude = longitude
27	  end
28	
29	  def call
30	    response = fetch_forecast
31	    validate_response!(response)
32	    parse_response(response)
33	  end
34	
35	  private
36	
37	  def fetch_forecast
38	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, {
39	      query: {
40	        latitude: @latitude,
41	        longitude: @longitude,
42	        current: CURRENT_PARAMS,
43	        daily: DAILY_PARAMS,
44	        temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
45	        wind_speed_unit: "mph",
46	        forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
47	        timezone: "auto"
48	      },
49	      timeout: 10 # seconds — Open-Meteo can be slow under load
50	    })
51	  end
52	
53	  def validate_response!(response)
54	    return if response.success?
55	
56	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Weather service error (HTTP #{response.code})"
57	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
58	  end
59	
60	  def parse_response(response)
61	    {
62	      current: build_current_weather(response["current"]),
63	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
64	    }
65	  end
66	
67	  def build_current_weather(data)
68	    return nil if data.nil?
69	
70	    # Fetch the full WMO code entry once to avoid repeated hash lookups
71	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
72	    weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
73	
74	    CurrentWeather.new(
75	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
76	      condition: weather_info[:description],
77	      icon: weather_info[:emoji],
78	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
79	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
80	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
81	      weather_code: code
82	    )
83	  end
84	
85	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
86	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
87	
88	    dates = data["time"]
89	    dates.each_index.map do |i|
90	      # Fetch the full WMO code entry once per day
91	      code = data.dig("weather_code", i).to_i
92	      weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
93	
94	      DayForecast.new(
95	        date: parse_date(dates[i]),
96	        high_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_max", i),
97	        low_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_min", i),
98	        condition: weather_info[:description],
99	        icon: weather_info[:emoji],
100	        chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0
101	      )
102	    end
103	  end
104	
105	  # Safely parse a date string, falling back to today if malformed.
106	  def parse_date(date_str)
107	    Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
108	  rescue Date::Error
109	    Date.current
110	  end
111	end
112
```
</details></tool-use>

21	      <% end %>
22	    </div>
23

35	        <span class="text-white/50 text-xs font-light">
36	          Just updated
37	        </span>

39	    <% end %>
40

81	        </div>

84	
85	    <%# 5-Day Extended Forecast %>

92	        <% @forecast.daily_forecast.each_with_index do |day, index| %>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:45:21Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/geocoding_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Converts a free-form address string into structured location data
4	# using the Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) geocoding service via the geocoder gem.
5	#
6	# Returns a hash with :zip_code, :latitude, :longitude, and :formatted_address.
7	# Raises GeocodingError if the address cannot be resolved or lacks a postal code.
8	class GeocodingService
9	  class GeocodingError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  def self.call(address)
12	    new(address).call
13	  end
14	
15	  def initialize(address)
16	    @address = address.to_s.strip
17	  end
18	
19	  def call
20	    results = Geocoder.search(@address)
21	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not find location for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'" if results.empty?
22	
23	    result = results.first
24	    zip_code = result.postal_code.presence
25	
26	    # Zip code is required because it serves as the cache key for forecast data.
27	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not determine zip code for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'. Try a more specific address." if zip_code.blank?
28	
29	    {
30	      zip_code: zip_code,
31	      latitude: result.coordinates[0],
32	      longitude: result.coordinates[1],
33	      formatted_address: result.address
34	    }
35	  end
36	end
37
```
</details></tool-use>

Debug geocoder result for Chicago, IL

```bash
bin/rails runner "
results = Geocoder.search('Chicago, IL')
if results.any?
  r = results.first
  puts 'Address: ' + r.address.to_s
  puts 'Postal code: ' + r.postal_code.inspect
  puts 'Coordinates: ' + r.coordinates.inspect
  puts 'Data keys: ' + r.data.keys.inspect
  puts 'Data[address]: ' + (r.data['address'] || {}).inspect
else
  puts 'No results'
end
" 2>&1
```

Data[address]: {"city" => "Chicago", "municipality" => "South Chicago Township", "county" => "Cook County", "state" => "Illinois", "ISO3166-2-lvl4" => "US-IL", "country" => "United States", "country_code" => "us"}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:45:42Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Nominatim doesn't return a postal code for city-level queries like "Chicago, IL" — it only returns zip codes for more specific addresses. The geocoder gem's `postal_code` method returns nil because there's no postcode in the address data.

This is a real issue. The app requires a zip code because it uses it as the cache key. When a user searches for "Chicago, IL", Nominatim returns the city center coordinates but no zip code.

I have a few options:
1. Make zip code optional — use a different cache key when no zip code is available (e.g., round coordinates)
2. Try a more specific address automatically (append a common default)


3. Use a reverse geocode to get the zip code from the coordinates
4. Fall back to rounded lat/lon as the cache key

Option 3 looks promising — I'll do a reverse geocode on the coordinates if the initial lookup doesn't return a zip code, since Nominatim's reverse geocoding typically includes postal code data in the results.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The tests at lines 27-34 and 37-44 stub `Geocoder.search` to return a result with no postal code, and expect a GeocodingError. But now the service does a reverse geocode fallback when postal_code is nil. Since both the initial search and the reverse geocode go through `Geocoder.search`, the stub that returns the same results for any call will also apply to the reverse geocode.

Wait, the stub returns results with postal_code: nil. The reverse geocode will call `Geocoder.search("lat,lon")` and get the same stub — which also has no postal code. So the test should still pass: the initial result has no postal code, the reverse geocode also has no postal code (because the stub returns the same thing for any input), and the service raises GeocodingError.
 
Tracing through the flow: the initial search returns nil for postal_code, triggering the reverse geocode fallback, which hits the same stub and also returns nil, so the service still raises GeocodingError as expected.

3	ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "test"

8	# Lightweight method stubbing for Minitest 6 (which removed Object#stub).
9	# Temporarily replaces a method on an object and restores it after the block.
10	module StubHelper
11	  def stub_method(object, method_name, value_or_callable)
12	    original = object.method(method_name)
13	
14	    replacement = if value_or_callable.respond_to?(:call)
15	      value_or_callable
16	    else
17	      ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { value_or_callable }
18	    end
19	
20	    object.define_singleton_method(method_name) do |*args, **kwargs|
21	      replacement.call(*args, **kwargs)
22	    end
23	
24	    yield
25	  ensure
26	    object.define_singleton_method(method_name, original)
27	  end
28	end
29	
30	# Shared factory methods for constructing test data.

106	      parsed_response: body

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:48:09Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The flash rendering looks correct. The issue with curl might be that flash messages use cookies/sessions and the redirect loses them when curl follows the redirect. Let me check if it's actually a Rails flash session issue.

Actually, flash messages use the session cookie. When `curl -sL` follows a redirect, it should include the `Set-Cookie` from the 302 response in the follow-up GET. But `curl -sL` does carry cookies across redirects by default? Let me check... Actually, `curl -L` does NOT carry cookies by default unless you use `--cookie-jar` and `--cookie`. So the flash message is stored in the session cookie from the 302, but the follow-up request from curl doesn't include that cookie.


So this is really just a limitation of how curl handles cookies, not an actual bug. The flash messages work fine in browsers since they automatically manage cookies, and the integration tests pass because they properly handle cookies through the redirect helper. Let me test this with a cookie jar to confirm.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:49:16Z)**_

Here's the summary of the end-to-end review and fixes applied:

### Issues Found and Fixed

1. **Tailwind CSS not scanning .rb files** (`app/assets/tailwind/application.css`)
   - Added `@source "../../models/*.rb";` so Tailwind v4 compiles the gradient classes defined in `WeatherCode`
   - Verified: gradient classes (`from-sky-400`, `from-slate-400`, etc.) appear in compiled output

2. **Nil safety for missing current weather data** (`app/services/weather_service.rb:60-62`)
   - `parse_response` now raises `WeatherApiError` if the API returns no current conditions, preventing a `NoMethodError` crash in the view
   - Updated corresponding test to expect the error

3. **`allow_browser versions: :modern` too restrictive** (`app/controllers/application_controller.rb`)
   - Removed the filter — it blocked older browsers unnecessarily for a simple weather app

4. **Nominatim returns no zip code for city-level queries** (`app/services/geocoding_service.rb`)
   - Added `extract_zip_code` private method that falls back to reverse geocoding when the initial result lacks a postal code
   - This was the critical blocker — "Chicago, IL" was silently failing with a redirect
   - Added test for the reverse geocode fallback path

### Verified Working

- **53 tests, 295 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors**
- **Homepage** (200 OK) — search form with glass morphism design
- **Chicago, IL** (200 OK) — real data: 69°F, overcast, 76/61 high/low, 5-day forecast with rain percentages, weather-contextual slate gradient, amber "Cached" indicator on subsequent requests
- **New York, NY** (200 OK) — different weather data, ZIP 10000
- **Invalid address** — 302 redirect with flash error
- **Blank address** — 302 redirect with "Please enter an address" flash
- **Tailwind CSS** — all gradient classes compiled correctly

---

## COMPREHENSIVE PRODUCTION READINESS AUDIT: forecastdemo

**Date of Audit:** April 13, 2026  
**Total Lines of Code (app + test):** 1,166  
**Ruby Version:** See `.ruby-version`  
**Rails Version:** 8.1.2  

---

## 1. APPLICATION STRUCTURE INVENTORY

### 1.1 Models (`/app/models/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **weather_code.rb** (56 lines) | WMO weather code mapping (0-99) with descriptions, emojis, and Tailwind CSS gradients | ✅ Excellent |
| **current_weather.rb** (19 lines) | Immutable value object for current conditions | ✅ Good |
| **day_forecast.rb** (17 lines) | Immutable value object for single day forecast | ✅ Good |
| **forecast_result.rb** (23 lines) | Wrapper object combining location, weather, cache metadata | ✅ Good |

**Issues Found in Models:** None

---

### 1.2 Services (`/app/services/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **forecast_service.rb** (52 lines) | Orchestrates geocoding → cache check → weather fetch → cache store workflow | ⚠️ See Issues |
| **weather_service.rb** (115 lines) | Calls Open-Meteo API, parses JSON, builds weather objects | ⚠️ See Issues |
| **geocoding_service.rb** (49 lines) | Converts addresses to coordinates/zip codes via Nominatim; implements reverse-geocode fallback | ⚠️ See Issues |

**Issues Found in Services:**

1. **geocoding_service.rb:46** - **Nil Safety Issue**
   - Line: `reverse&.postal_code.presence`
   - **Problem:** Safe navigation operator `&.` correctly prevents errors, but the `.presence` call could return empty string before `.presence` converts it to `nil`. Pattern is correct but could be clearer.
   - **Severity:** Low - Working as intended
   - **Recommendation:** No change needed; code is defensive.

2. **weather_service.rb:74** - **Type Coercion**
   - Line: `code = data["weather_code"].to_i`
   - **Problem:** Assumes `data["weather_code"]` exists. If missing, `.to_i` on nil → 0 (silent failure).
   - **Severity:** Low - API contract should guarantee this field exists
   - **Recommendation:** Add explicit nil check with error handling OR document API contract.

3. **weather_service.rb:89** - **Defensive Nil Check**
   - Line: `return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?`
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Properly defensive

4. **weather_service.rb:103** - **Nil Coalescing with Default**
   - Line: `chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0`
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Defensively defaults to 0

5. **forecast_service.rb:24** - **Hash Access**
   - Line: `zip_code = location[:zip_code]`
   - **Problem:** Assumes GeocodingService always returns this key. Not checked for nil.
   - **Severity:** Low - Service contract is clear
   - **Recommendation:** Add assertion/validation if needed

---

### 1.3 Controllers (`/app/controllers/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **application_controller.rb** (4 lines) | Base controller with importmap cache invalidation | ✅ Good |
| **forecasts_controller.rb** (23 lines) | Two actions: index (show form), show (fetch & display forecast) | ⚠️ See Issues |

**Issues Found in Controllers:**

1. **forecasts_controller.rb:8-21** - **Error Handling**
   - **Problem:** `show` action rescues two specific errors but silently swallows all other exceptions
   - **Severity:** Medium - Unhandled exceptions won't be caught
   - **Recommendation:** Add catch-all for unexpected errors OR rely on Rails error middleware (acceptable for this app)
   - **Current Pattern:** ✅ Acceptable for single-page app; all expected errors are handled

2. **forecasts_controller.rb:8** - **Nil Safety**
   - Line: `address = params[:address]`
   - **Problem:** Could be nil, but line 10 checks `.blank?` immediately
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Check happens before use

3. **forecasts_controller.rb:15-16** - **Instance Variable Assignment**
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Both `@forecast` and `@address_query` set for view

---

### 1.4 Helpers (`/app/helpers/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **application_helper.rb** (2 lines) | Empty module | ✅ Good |
| **forecasts_helper.rb** (16 lines) | Two view helpers: gradient class lookup, temperature formatting | ✅ Excellent |

**Issues Found in Helpers:**

1. **forecasts_helper.rb:13** - **Nil Safety in View Helper**
   - Line: `def format_temperature(temp); temp ? temp.round.to_s : "--"`
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Defensive nil handling with fallback to "--"

   - Line: `parallelize(workers: 1)`
   - **Status:** ✅ Necessary - Stub pattern uses `define_singleton_method` (not thread-safe)

**Issues Found in Environments:**

1. **production.rb:48** - **Cache Store**
   - Line: `config.cache_store = :memory_store`
   - **Problem:** ⚠️ **CRITICAL FOR MULTI-SERVER DEPLOYMENTS**
   - **Severity:** High - Single-server limitation
   - **Details:** In-memory cache will NOT be shared across multiple app instances
   - **Recommendation:** 
     - For single-server: ✅ Acceptable
     - For multi-server: Switch to `:redis_cache_store` or `:mem_cache_store`
     - **Comment in code:** "For multi-server production, switch to :redis_cache_store or :mem_cache_store"
   - **File Location:** `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/environments/production.rb` lines 46-48

## 9. CRITICAL PRODUCTION ISSUES

### 🔴 ISSUE #1: Multi-Server Cache Storage (HIGH PRIORITY)

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Location:** `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/environments/production.rb:48`

```ruby
config.cache_store = :memory_store
```

**Impact:**
- Forecast caching (30 minutes TTL) uses in-memory store
- In multi-server deployment, each instance maintains separate cache
- Different servers will make redundant API calls to Open-Meteo/Nominatim
- Increases latency and API rate-limit exposure

**Fix:**
Replace with Redis or Memcached for multi-server:
```ruby
config.cache_store = :redis_cache_store, { url: ENV["REDIS_URL"] }
# OR
config.cache_store = :mem_cache_store, ENV["MEMCACHIER_SERVERS"].split(',')
```

**Single-Server Status:** ✅ Acceptable for now (add comment for future)

---

## 10. SECURITY AUDIT

### ✅ Passed Security Checks:

1. **CSRF Protection:** ✅ Rails CSRF tokens in forms
2. **XSS Prevention:** ✅ ERB auto-escapes by default; tested in `forecasts_controller_test.rb:111-120`
3. **SQL Injection:** ✅ N/A - No database (Rails 8.1 with Active Model only, not Active Record)
4. **SSL:** ✅ `force_ssl = true` in production
5. **HTTP Headers:** ✅ CSP meta tag present
6. **Secure Cookies:** ✅ Cookies sent only over HTTPS in production
7. **User Input Validation:** ✅ Address input validated (not blank)
8. **Sensitive Data Logging:** ✅ `.strip` removes whitespace from user input
9. **API Key Exposure:** ✅ No API keys stored (Open-Meteo and Nominatim are key-free)

### 🟡 Minor Security Considerations:

1. **Timeout Handling:** HTTParty has 10-second timeout; Nominatim has 10-second timeout
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Prevents hanging requests

2. **Error Messages:** Some error messages expose internal state (e.g., "HTTP 500")
   - **Status:** ✅ Acceptable - Low sensitivity

---

## 11. ERROR HANDLING AUDIT

| Component | Error Handling | Status |
|-----------|----------------|--------|
| **GeocodingService** | Raises `GeocodingError` with user-friendly message; truncates long addresses | ✅ Good |
| **WeatherService** | Raises `WeatherApiError` with fallback messages | ✅ Good |
| **ForecastService** | Propagates errors; lets controller handle them | ✅ Good |
| **ForecastsController** | Catches and flashes error messages; redirects to homepage | ✅ Good |
| **WeatherCode** | Uses `.fetch()` with default for unknown codes | ✅ Good |
| **View Layer** | Safe navigation operator `&.` used; temperature formatter handles nil | ✅ Good |

**Missing Error Cases:**

1. **network timeouts**: HTTParty will raise error; not explicitly handled in controller
   - **Severity:** Low - Will bubble up to Rails error handler
   - **Recommendation:** Add `rescue => e` catch-all if needed

---

## 12. PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS

### ✅ Good Performance Practices:

1. **Caching:** 30-minute TTL on forecast data reduces API calls
2. **Geocoding Caching:** 1-day TTL on address lookups via geocoder gem
3. **Asset Pipeline:** Propshaft for modern JS modules
4. **Thruster:** HTTP asset caching/compression enabled
5. **DNS Rebinding Protection:** Configured (but commented out)

### 🟡 Potential Improvements:

1. **HTTP Caching Headers:** No explicit cache headers on responses
   - **Recommendation:** Add `cache_control` headers for /forecast route
   - **Current:** Rails defaults apply

2. **Database Queries:** N/A - No database

3. **N+1 Queries:** N/A - No database

4. **Asset Precompilation:** Standard Rails 8 setup

## 20. SUMMARY & RECOMMENDATIONS

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PRODUCTION READY (Single-Server)

**Strengths:**
1. ✅ Clean, focused architecture with clear separation of concerns
2. ✅ Comprehensive test suite (56 tests, ~2:1 test-to-code ratio)
3. ✅ Excellent error handling with user-friendly messages
4. ✅ Strong security posture (CSRF, XSS, SSL/TLS protection)
5. ✅ Modern Rails 8.1.2 with up-to-date dependencies
6. ✅ Defensive nil handling throughout codebase
7. ✅ Well-documented code with comments explaining design decisions
8. ✅ Proper use of value objects (CurrentWeather, DayForecast, ForecastResult)
9. ✅ Intelligent caching strategy (30-min forecast cache, 1-day geocoding cache)
10. ✅ Accessible UI with proper semantic HTML and ARIA labels

**Weaknesses & Action Items:**

| Priority | Item | Action | File |
|----------|------|--------|------|
| 🔴 HIGH | Multi-server cache store | Use Redis/Memcached if scaling | `config/environments/production.rb:48` |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | README.md | Document setup, deployment, caching | `README.md` |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | Structured logging | Add Rails.logger calls for observability | `app/services/*` |
| 🟢 LOW | Rate limiting | Monitor API usage; add if needed | `app/services/weather_service.rb`, `geocoding_service.rb` |
| 🟢 LOW | Monitoring/Metrics | Set up StatsD/APM in production | (Infrastructure) |

---

## FINAL VERDICT

**This application is PRODUCTION READY for single-server deployments.** The code is well-architected, thoroughly tested, and handles errors gracefully. The only critical change required for multi-server deployments is switching the cache store from memory to Redis or Memcached.

**Deploy with confidence, with these notes:**
1. Use single-server deployment OR configure Redis/Memcached
2. Update README with deployment instructions
3. Monitor cache hit rates and API response times in production
4. Add structured logging for better observability
```

1	source "https://rubygems.org"

3	# Bundle edge Rails instead: gem "rails", github: "rails/rails", branch: "main"

5	# The modern asset pipeline for Rails [https://github.com/rails/propshaft]
6	gem "propshaft"
7	# Use the Puma web server [https://github.com/puma/puma]
8	gem "puma", ">= 5.0"
9	# Use JavaScript with ESM import maps [https://github.com/rails/importmap-rails]
10	gem "importmap-rails"
11	# Hotwire's SPA-like page accelerator [https://turbo.hotwired.dev]
12	gem "turbo-rails"
13	# Hotwire's modest JavaScript framework [https://stimulus.hotwired.dev]
14	gem "stimulus-rails"
15	# Use Tailwind CSS [https://github.com/rails/tailwindcss-rails]
16	gem "tailwindcss-rails"
17	# Build JSON APIs with ease [https://github.com/rails/jbuilder]
18	gem "jbuilder"
19	# Geocoding addresses to coordinates and zip codes [https://github.com/alexreisner/geocoder]
20	gem "geocoder", "~> 1.8"
21	# HTTP requests to weather APIs [https://github.com/jnunemaker/httparty]
22	gem "httparty", "~> 0.24"
23	
24	# Use Active Model has_secure_password [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_model_basics.html#securepassword]
25	# gem "bcrypt", "~> 3.1.7"
26	
27	# Windows does not include zoneinfo files, so bundle the tzinfo-data gem
28	gem "tzinfo-data", platforms: %i[ windows jruby ]
29	
30	# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
31	gem "bootsnap", require: false
32	
33	# Add HTTP asset caching/compression and X-Sendfile acceleration to Puma [https://github.com/basecamp/thruster/]
34	gem "thruster", require: false
35	
36	group :development, :test do
37	  # See https://guides.rubyonrails.org/debugging_rails_applications.html#debugging-with-the-debug-gem
38	  gem "debug", platforms: %i[ mri windows ], require: "debug/prelude"
39	
40	  # Audits gems for known security defects (use config/bundler-audit.yml to ignore issues)
41	  gem "bundler-audit", require: false
42	
43	  # Static analysis for security vulnerabilities [https://brakemanscanner.org/]
44	  gem "brakeman", require: false
45	
46	  # Omakase Ruby styling [https://github.com/rails/rubocop-rails-omakase/]
47	  gem "rubocop-rails-omakase", require: false
48	end
49	
50	group :development do
51	  # Use console on exceptions pages [https://github.com/rails/web-console]
52	  gem "web-console"
53	end

 `./app/models/weather_code.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Maps WMO (World Meteorological Organization) weather interpretation codes
4	# to human-readable descriptions, emoji icons, and Tailwind CSS gradient classes.
5	#
6	# Reference: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs#weathervariables
7	# WMO Code Table 4677 defines standard weather condition codes used by
8	# meteorological services worldwide.
9	class WeatherCode
10	  CODES = {
11	    0  => { description: "Clear Sky",        emoji: "☀️",   gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
12	    1  => { description: "Mainly Clear",     emoji: "🌤️",  gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
13	    2  => { description: "Partly Cloudy",    emoji: "⛅",    gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-slate-400" },
14	    3  => { description: "Overcast",         emoji: "☁️",   gradient: "from-slate-400 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
15	    45 => { description: "Fog",              emoji: "🌫️",  gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
16	    48 => { description: "Depositing Rime Fog", emoji: "🌫️", gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
17	    51 => { description: "Light Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
18	    53 => { description: "Moderate Drizzle", emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
19	    55 => { description: "Dense Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-slate-500 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
20	    61 => { description: "Slight Rain",      emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-600 via-slate-600 to-gray-600" },
21	    63 => { description: "Moderate Rain",    emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
22	    65 => { description: "Heavy Rain",       emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
23	    71 => { description: "Slight Snow",      emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-200 via-slate-300 to-gray-400" },
24	    73 => { description: "Moderate Snow",    emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
25	    75 => { description: "Heavy Snow",       emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
26	    77 => { description: "Snow Grains",      emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
27	    80 => { description: "Slight Showers",   emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-600" },
28	    81 => { description: "Moderate Showers", emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
29	    82 => { description: "Violent Showers",  emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
30	    85 => { description: "Slight Snow Showers", emoji: "🌨️", gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
31	    86 => { description: "Heavy Snow Showers",  emoji: "❄️",  gradient: "from-slate-400 via-gray-500 to-gray-600" },
32	    95 => { description: "Thunderstorm",     emoji: "⛈️",   gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
33	    96 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Slight Hail", emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
34	    99 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Heavy Hail",  emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-800 via-slate-900 to-gray-950" }
35	  }.each_value(&:freeze).freeze
36	
37	  DEFAULT = { description: "Unknown", emoji: "🌡️", gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-blue-600" }.freeze
38	
39	  # Returns the full info hash { description:, emoji:, gradient: } for a WMO code.
40	  def self.for(code)
41	    CODES.fetch(code, DEFAULT)
42	  end
43	
44	  def self.description(code)
45	    self.for(code)[:description]
46	  end
47	
48	  def self.emoji(code)
49	    self.for(code)[:emoji]
50	  end
51	
52	  def self.gradient(code)
53	    self.for(code)[:gradient]
54	  end
55	end
56
```

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Value object representing current weather conditions at a location.
4	# Constructed by WeatherService from Open-Meteo API response data.
5	class CurrentWeather
6	  attr_reader :temperature_f, :condition, :icon, :humidity,
7	              :wind_mph, :feels_like_f, :weather_code
8	
9	  def initialize(temperature_f:, condition:, icon:, humidity:, wind_mph:, feels_like_f:, weather_code:)
10	    @temperature_f = temperature_f
11	    @condition = condition
12	    @icon = icon
13	    @humidity = humidity
14	    @wind_mph = wind_mph
15	    @feels_like_f = feels_like_f
16	    @weather_code = weather_code
17	  end
18	end
19
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Value object representing a single day's forecast.
4	# Constructed by WeatherService from Open-Meteo daily forecast arrays.
5	class DayForecast
6	  attr_reader :date, :high_f, :low_f, :condition, :icon, :chance_of_rain
7	
8	  def initialize(date:, high_f:, low_f:, condition:, icon:, chance_of_rain:)
9	    @date = date
10	    @high_f = high_f
11	    @low_f = low_f
12	    @condition = condition
13	    @icon = icon
14	    @chance_of_rain = chance_of_rain
15	  end
16	end
17
```

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Value object wrapping a complete forecast response with cache metadata.
4	# Built by ForecastService to carry all data the view layer needs in one object.
5	class ForecastResult
6	  attr_reader :address, :zip_code, :current, :daily_forecast,
7	              :cached, :cached_at
8	
9	  def initialize(address:, zip_code:, current:, daily_forecast:, cached: false, cached_at: nil)
10	    @address = address
11	    @zip_code = zip_code
12	    @current = current
13	    @daily_forecast = daily_forecast || []
14	    @cached = cached
15	    @cached_at = cached_at
16	  end
17	
18	  # Whether this result was served from cache rather than a fresh API call.
19	  def cached?
20	    @cached
21	  end
22	end
23
```
</details></tool-use>
