Now let me read the services:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 00:49:56Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/forecast_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Orchestrates the full forecast workflow: geocode -> cache check -> fetch -> cache store.
4	#
5	# Caching strategy:
6	# - Cache key is based on zip code (e.g., "forecast_v1/60601")
7	# - TTL is 30 minutes per the requirements
8	# - Uses Rails.cache.read/write (not .fetch) so we can distinguish cache hits from misses
9	#   and pass the `cached` flag + `cached_at` timestamp to the view layer
10	class ForecastService
11	  CACHE_EXPIRY = 30.minutes
12	  CACHE_PREFIX = "forecast_v1"
13	
14	  def self.call(address)
15	    new(address).call
16	  end
17	
18	  def initialize(address)
19	    @address = address
20	  end
21	
22	  def call
23	    location = GeocodingService.call(@address)
24	    zip_code = location[:zip_code]
25	    cache_key = "#{CACHE_PREFIX}/#{zip_code}"
26	
27	    cached_data = Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
28	
29	    if cached_data
30	      build_result(location, cached_data[:weather], zip_code, cached: true, cached_at: cached_data[:fetched_at])
31	    else
32	      weather = WeatherService.call(latitude: location[:latitude], longitude: location[:longitude])
33	      fetched_at = Time.current
34	      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, { weather: weather, fetched_at: fetched_at }, expires_in: CACHE_EXPIRY)
35	      build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached: false, cached_at: fetched_at)
36	    end
37	  end
38	
39	  private
40	
41	  def build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached:, cached_at:)
42	    ForecastResult.new(
43	      address: location[:formatted_address],
44	      zip_code: zip_code,
45	      current: weather[:current],
46	      daily_forecast: weather[:daily_forecast],
47	      cached: cached,
48	      cached_at: cached_at
49	    )
50	  end
51	end
52
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 00:49:56Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/weather_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Fetches weather data from the Open-Meteo API (https://open-meteo.com).
4	# No API key required. Returns current conditions and a multi-day forecast.
5	#
6	# Open-Meteo uses WMO weather interpretation codes (mapped by WeatherCode).
7	# API docs: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs
8	class WeatherService
9	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
12	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
13	
14	  # Current weather fields requested from Open-Meteo
15	  CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
16	
17	  # Daily forecast fields requested from Open-Meteo
18	  DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
19	
20	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
21	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
22	  end
23	
24	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
25	    @latitude = latitude
26	    @longitude = longitude
27	  end
28	
29	  def call
30	    response = fetch_forecast
31	    validate_response!(response)
32	    parse_response(response)
33	  end
34	
35	  private
36	
37	  def fetch_forecast
38	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, {
39	      query: {
40	        latitude: @latitude,
41	        longitude: @longitude,
42	        current: CURRENT_PARAMS,
43	        daily: DAILY_PARAMS,
44	        temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
45	        wind_speed_unit: "mph",
46	        forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
47	        timezone: "auto"
48	      },
49	      timeout: 10 # seconds — Open-Meteo can be slow under load
50	    })
51	  end
52	
53	  def validate_response!(response)
54	    return if response.success?
55	
56	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Weather service error (HTTP #{response.code})"
57	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
58	  end
59	
60	  def parse_response(response)
61	    current = build_current_weather(response["current"])
62	    raise WeatherApiError, "Weather data unavailable — no current conditions returned" if current.nil?
63	
64	    {
65	      current: current,
66	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
67	    }
68	  end
69	
70	  def build_current_weather(data)
71	    return nil if data.nil?
72	
73	    # Fetch the full WMO code entry once to avoid repeated hash lookups
74	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
75	    weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
76	
77	    CurrentWeather.new(
78	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
79	      condition: weather_info[:description],
80	      icon: weather_info[:emoji],
81	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
82	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
83	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
84	      weather_code: code
85	    )
86	  end
87	
88	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
89	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
90	
91	    dates = data["time"]
92	    dates.each_index.map do |i|
93	      # Fetch the full WMO code entry once per day
94	      code = data.dig("weather_code", i).to_i
95	      weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
96	
97	      DayForecast.new(
98	        date: parse_date(dates[i]),
99	        high_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_max", i),
100	        low_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_min", i),
101	        condition: weather_info[:description],
102	        icon: weather_info[:emoji],
103	        chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0
104	      )
105	    end
106	  end
107	
108	  # Safely parse a date string, falling back to today if malformed.
109	  def parse_date(date_str)
110	    Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
111	  rescue Date::Error
112	    Date.current
113	  end
114	end
115
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/geocoding_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Converts a free-form address string into structured location data
4	# using the Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) geocoding service via the geocoder gem.
5	#
6	# Returns a hash with :zip_code, :latitude, :longitude, and :formatted_address.
7	# Raises GeocodingError if the address cannot be resolved or lacks a postal code.
8	class GeocodingService
9	  class GeocodingError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  def self.call(address)
12	    new(address).call
13	  end
14	
15	  def initialize(address)
16	    @address = address.to_s.strip
17	  end
18	
19	  def call
20	    results = Geocoder.search(@address)
21	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not find location for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'" if results.empty?
22	
23	    result = results.first
24	    zip_code = extract_zip_code(result)
25	
26	    # Zip code is required because it serves as the cache key for forecast data.
27	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not determine zip code for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'. Try a more specific address." if zip_code.blank?
28	
29	    {
30	      zip_code: zip_code,
31	      latitude: result.coordinates[0],
32	      longitude: result.coordinates[1],
33	      formatted_address: result.address
34	    }
35	  end
36	
37	  private
38	
39	  # Nominatim omits postal codes for city-level queries. When that happens,
40	  # reverse-geocode the coordinates to get a zip code for the city center.
41	  def extract_zip_code(result)
42	    return result.postal_code if result.postal_code.present?
43	
44	    lat, lon = result.coordinates
45	    reverse = Geocoder.search("#{lat},#{lon}").first
46	    reverse&.postal_code.presence
47	  end
48	end
49
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	class ForecastsController < ApplicationController
4	  def index
5	  end
6	
7	  def show
8	    address = params[:address]
9	
10	    if address.blank?
11	      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Please enter an address."
12	      return
13	    end
14	
15	    @forecast = ForecastService.call(address)
16	    @address_query = address
17	  rescue GeocodingService::GeocodingError => e
18	    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
19	  rescue WeatherService::WeatherApiError => e
20	    redirect_to root_path, alert: "Weather data unavailable: #{e.message}"
21	  end
22	end
23
```

12	  },
13	  timeout: 10, # seconds — Nominatim can be slow under heavy load
14	  cache: Rails.cache,
15	  cache_options: {
16	    expiration: 1.day, # Geocoding results rarely change; cache aggressively
17	    prefix: "geocoder:"
18	  }

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "test"
4	require_relative "../config/environment"
5	require "rails/test_help"
6	require "ostruct"
7	
8	# Lightweight method stubbing for Minitest 6 (which removed Object#stub).
9	# Temporarily replaces a method on an object and restores it after the block.
10	module StubHelper
11	  def stub_method(object, method_name, value_or_callable)
12	    original = object.method(method_name)
13	
14	    replacement = if value_or_callable.respond_to?(:call)
15	      value_or_callable
16	    else
17	      ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { value_or_callable }
18	    end
19	
20	    object.define_singleton_method(method_name) do |*args, **kwargs|
21	      replacement.call(*args, **kwargs)
22	    end
23	
24	    yield
25	  ensure
26	    object.define_singleton_method(method_name, original)
27	  end
28	end
29

55	        DayForecast.new(date: Date.today + 1,  high_f: 75.0, low_f: 60.0, condition: "Mainly Clear",  icon: "🌤️", chance_of_rain: 5),
56	        DayForecast.new(date: Date.today + 2,  high_f: 80.0, low_f: 65.0, condition: "Clear Sky",     icon: "☀️",  chance_of_rain: 0)
57	      ]

84	        "weather_code" => [ 2, 1, 0 ],
85	        "temperature_2m_max" => [ 78.0, 75.0, 80.0 ],
86	        "temperature_2m_min" => [ 62.0, 60.0, 65.0 ],
87	        "precipitation_probability_max" => [ 10, 5, 0 ]
88	      }
89	    }
90	  end
91	
92	  def stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: "60601", coordinates: [ 41.8781, -87.6298 ], address: "Chicago, Cook County, Illinois, 60601, United States")
93	    mock = OpenStruct.new(
94	      postal_code: postal_code,
95	      coordinates: coordinates,
96	      address: address
97	    )
98	    [ mock ]
99	  end
100	
101	  # Builds a mock HTTParty response object with the essential interface.
102	  def mock_http_response(code, body)
103	    response = OpenStruct.new(
104	      code: code,
105	      success?: code == 200,
106	      parsed_response: body
107	    )
108	    response.define_singleton_method(:dig) { |*keys| body.dig(*keys) }
109	    response.define_singleton_method(:[]) { |key| body[key] }
110	    response
111	  end
112	end
113	
114	module ActiveSupport
115	  class TestCase
116	    # Run tests in single process — our stub approach uses define_singleton_method
117	    # which is not thread-safe across parallel workers.
118	    parallelize(workers: 1)
119	
120	    include WeatherTestHelper
121	    include StubHelper
122	  end
123	end
124
```
</details></tool-use>

3	require "test_helper"

5	class ForecastServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
6	  setup do
7	    Rails.cache.clear
8	  end
9	
10	  test "returns a fresh ForecastResult on first request" do
11	    location = build_mock_location
12	    weather = build_mock_weather
13	
14	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
15	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kw) { weather }) do
16	        result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
17	
18	        assert_instance_of ForecastResult, result
19	        assert_equal "60601", result.zip_code
20	        assert_not result.cached?
21	        assert_not_nil result.cached_at
22	        assert_equal 72.0, result.current.temperature_f
23	        assert_equal 3, result.daily_forecast.length
24	      end
25	    end
26	  end
27	
28	  test "returns cached ForecastResult on second request for same zip" do
29	    location = build_mock_location
30	    weather = build_mock_weather
31	    weather_call_count = 0
32	
33	    weather_stub = ->(**_kw) { weather_call_count += 1; weather }
34	
35	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
36	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, weather_stub) do
37	        first = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
38	        assert_not first.cached?
39	
40	        second = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
41	        assert second.cached?
42	        assert_equal 1, weather_call_count, "WeatherService should only be called once"
43	      end
44	    end
45	  end
46	
47	  test "shares cache across different addresses with the same zip code" do
48	    location = build_mock_location
49	    weather = build_mock_weather
50	    weather_call_count = 0
51	
52	    weather_stub = ->(**_kw) { weather_call_count += 1; weather }
53	
54	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
55	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, weather_stub) do
56	        ForecastService.call("123 Main St, Chicago, IL")
57	        result = ForecastService.call("456 Oak Ave, Chicago, IL")
58	
59	        assert result.cached?
60	        assert_equal 1, weather_call_count
61	      end
62	    end
63	  end
64	
65	  test "cache expires after 30 minutes" do
66	    location = build_mock_location
67	    weather = build_mock_weather
68	
69	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
70	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kw) { weather }) do
71	        first = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
72	        assert_not first.cached?
73	
74	        travel ForecastService::CACHE_EXPIRY + 1.minute
75	
76	        second = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
77	        assert_not second.cached?, "Should be fresh after cache expiry"
78	      end
79	    end
80	  end
81	
82	  test "does not expire cache before 30 minutes" do
83	    location = build_mock_location
84	    weather = build_mock_weather
85	
86	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
87	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kw) { weather }) do

89	
90	        travel ForecastService::CACHE_EXPIRY - 1.minute
91	
92	        result = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
93	        assert result.cached?, "Should still be cached before expiry"
94	      end
95	    end
96	  end
97	
98	  test "propagates GeocodingError from GeocodingService" do

100	      assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do

102	      end
103	    end
104	  end

106	  test "propagates WeatherApiError from WeatherService" do

108	
109	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do

111	        assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
112	          ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
113	        end
114	      end
115	    end
116	  end
117	
118	  test "sets cached_at timestamp on both fresh and cached results" do
119	    location = build_mock_location
120	    weather = build_mock_weather
121	
122	    stub_method(GeocodingService, :call, ->(_addr) { location }) do
123	      stub_method(WeatherService, :call, ->(**_kw) { weather }) do
124	        fresh = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")
125	        assert_not_nil fresh.cached_at
126	
127	        cached = ForecastService.call("Chicago, IL")

129	      end
130	    end
131	  end
132	
133	  test "uses different cache entries for different zip codes" do

1	# frozen_string_literal: true

3	require "test_helper"
4	
5	class WeatherServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
6	  test "parses a successful API response into structured weather data" do
7	    response = mock_http_response(200, build_mock_open_meteo_response)
8	
9	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
10	      result = WeatherService.call(latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298)
11	
12	      # Current weather
13	      assert_instance_of CurrentWeather, result[:current]
14	      assert_equal 72.0, result[:current].temperature_f
15	      assert_equal "Partly Cloudy", result[:current].condition
16	      assert_equal "⛅", result[:current].icon
17	      assert_equal 45, result[:current].humidity
18	      assert_equal 8.5, result[:current].wind_mph
19	      assert_equal 70.0, result[:current].feels_like_f
20	      assert_equal 2, result[:current].weather_code
21	
22	      # Daily forecast
23	      assert_equal 3, result[:daily_forecast].length
24	      first_day = result[:daily_forecast].first
25	      assert_instance_of DayForecast, first_day
26	      assert_equal Date.today, first_day.date
27	      assert_equal 78.0, first_day.high_f
28	      assert_equal 62.0, first_day.low_f
29	      assert_equal "Partly Cloudy", first_day.condition
30	      assert_equal 10, first_day.chance_of_rain
31	    end
32	  end
33	
34	  test "raises WeatherApiError on non-success HTTP response" do
35	    response = mock_http_response(400, { "reason" => "Bad request" })
36	
37	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
38	      error = assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
39	        WeatherService.call(latitude: 0, longitude: 0)
40	      end
41	      assert_includes error.message, "Bad request"
42	    end
43	  end
44	
45	  test "provides fallback error message when API reason is missing" do
46	    response = mock_http_response(500, {})
47	
48	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
49	      error = assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
50	        WeatherService.call(latitude: 0, longitude: 0)
51	      end
52	      assert_includes error.message, "HTTP 500"
53	    end
54	  end
55

65	  end
66

71	
72	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do

3	require "test_helper"
4	
5	class GeocodingServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
6	  test "returns location data for a valid US address" do
7	    results = stub_geocoder_result
8	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
9	      location = GeocodingService.call("Chicago, IL")
10	
11	      assert_equal "60601", location[:zip_code]
12	      assert_in_delta 41.8781, location[:latitude], 0.001
13	      assert_in_delta(-87.6298, location[:longitude], 0.001)
14	      assert_includes location[:formatted_address], "Chicago"
15	    end
16	  end
17	
18	  test "raises GeocodingError when no results are found" do
19	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { [] }) do
20	      error = assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
21	        GeocodingService.call("xyznonexistent")
22	      end
23	      assert_includes error.message, "Could not find location"
24	    end
25	  end
26	
27	  test "falls back to reverse geocode when initial result has no postal code" do
28	    # Forward geocode returns no postal code; reverse geocode returns one
29	    forward_result = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: nil)
30	    reverse_result = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: "60604")
31	    call_count = 0
32	
33	    geocoder_stub = ->(_addr) {
34	      call_count += 1
35	      call_count == 1 ? forward_result : reverse_result
36	    }
37	
38	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, geocoder_stub) do
39	      location = GeocodingService.call("Chicago, IL")
40	      assert_equal "60604", location[:zip_code]
41	      assert_equal 2, call_count
42	    end
43	  end
44	
45	  test "raises GeocodingError when both forward and reverse geocode lack postal code" do
46	    results = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: nil)
47	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
48	      error = assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
49	        GeocodingService.call("Somewhere without zip")
50	      end
51	      assert_includes error.message, "Could not determine zip code"
52	    end
53	  end
54	
55	  test "raises GeocodingError when postal code is blank" do
56	    results = stub_geocoder_result(postal_code: "")
57	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { results }) do
58	      assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do
59	        GeocodingService.call("Blank zip address")
60	      end
61	    end
62	  end
63	
64	  test "strips whitespace from address input" do
65	    results = stub_geocoder_result
66	    received_address = nil
67	
68	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(addr) { received_address = addr; results }) do
69	      GeocodingService.call("  Chicago, IL  ")
70	      assert_equal "Chicago, IL", received_address
71	    end
72	  end
73	
74	  test "truncates long addresses in error messages" do
75	    stub_method(Geocoder, :search, ->(_addr) { [] }) do
76	      long_address = "A" * 200
77	      error = assert_raises(GeocodingService::GeocodingError) do

79	      end
80	      # Error message should be truncated, not the full 200 chars

82	    end
83	  end

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	require "test_helper"
4	
5	class WeatherCodeTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
6	  test "returns correct descriptions for known weather codes" do
7	    assert_equal "Clear Sky",      WeatherCode.description(0)
8	    assert_equal "Partly Cloudy",  WeatherCode.description(2)
9	    assert_equal "Heavy Rain",     WeatherCode.description(65)
10	    assert_equal "Thunderstorm",   WeatherCode.description(95)
11	  end
12	
13	  test "returns a non-empty emoji for known weather codes" do
14	    [ 0, 1, 2, 3, 45, 61, 71, 95 ].each do |code|
15	      emoji = WeatherCode.emoji(code)
16	      assert emoji.present?, "Code #{code} should have an emoji"
17	    end
18	  end
19	
20	  test "returns valid Tailwind gradient classes for known weather codes" do
21	    [ 0, 3, 65, 95 ].each do |code|
22	      gradient = WeatherCode.gradient(code)
23	      assert_match(/\Afrom-\S+ via-\S+ to-\S+\z/, gradient, "Code #{code} gradient should be valid")
24	    end
25	  end
26	
27	  test "returns default values for unknown weather codes" do
28	    info = WeatherCode.for(999)
29	    assert_equal "Unknown", info[:description]
30	    assert info[:emoji].present?
31	    assert info[:gradient].present?
32	  end
33	
34	  test "returns default for negative weather codes" do
35	    assert_equal "Unknown", WeatherCode.description(-1)
36	  end
37	
38	  test "all defined codes have description, emoji, and gradient" do
39	    WeatherCode::CODES.each do |code, info|
40	      assert info[:description].present?, "Code #{code} missing description"
41	      assert info[:emoji].present?,       "Code #{code} missing emoji"
42	      assert info[:gradient].present?,    "Code #{code} missing gradient"
43	    end
44	  end
45	
46	  test "CODES hash is frozen and cannot be mutated" do
47	    assert WeatherCode::CODES.frozen?
48	    assert_raises(FrozenError) { WeatherCode::CODES[0] = {} }
49	  end
50	end
51
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# frozen_string_literal: true

3	require "test_helper"
4	
5	class ForecastFlowTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
6	  setup do
7	    Rails.cache.clear
8	  end
9	
10	  test "full flow: homepage -> search -> forecast display" do
11	    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)
12	
13	    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
14	      # 1. Visit the homepage
15	      get root_path
16	      assert_response :success
17	      assert_select "h1", /Weather/
18	      assert_select "input[name='address']"
19	
20	      # 2. Submit an address
21	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
22	      assert_response :success
23	
24	      # 3. Verify location info
25	      assert_match(/Chicago/, response.body)
26	      assert_match(/60601/, response.body)
27	
28	      # 4. Verify current conditions
29	      assert_match(/72/, response.body)
30	      assert_match(/Feels Like/, response.body)
31	      assert_match(/Humidity/, response.body)
32	      assert_match(/Wind/, response.body)
33	
34	      # 5. Verify extended forecast
35	      assert_match(/5-Day Forecast/, response.body)
36	      assert_match(/Today/, response.body)
37	
38	      # 6. Verify fresh data indicator
39	      assert_match(/Just updated/, response.body)
40	    end
41	  end
42	
43	  test "cached result flow shows amber cache indicator with timestamp" do
44	    fresh_forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)
45	    cached_forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: true)
46	    call_count = 0
47	
48	    service_stub = ->(_addr) {
49	      call_count += 1
50	      call_count == 1 ? fresh_forecast : cached_forecast
51	    }
52	
53	    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, service_stub) do
54	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
55	      assert_match(/Just updated/, response.body)
56	      assert_no_match(/Cached result from/, response.body)
57	
58	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
59	      assert_match(/Cached result from/, response.body)
60	      assert_no_match(/Just updated/, response.body)
61	    end
62	  end
63	
64	  test "error flow: invalid address shows flash and returns to search form" do
65	    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise GeocodingService::GeocodingError, "Could not find location" }) do
66	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "invalid" }
67	      assert_redirected_to root_path
68	
69	      follow_redirect!
70	      assert_response :success
71	      assert_match(/Could not find location/, response.body)
72	      assert_select "input[name='address']" # Search form still available
73	    end
74	  end
75	
76	  test "weather API error flow shows user-friendly message" do
77	    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { raise WeatherService::WeatherApiError, "Service timeout" }) do
78	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
79	      assert_redirected_to root_path
80	
81	      follow_redirect!
82	      assert_match(/Weather data unavailable/, response.body)
83	    end
84	  end
85	
86	  test "search form is present on the forecast results page for easy re-search" do
87	    forecast = build_mock_forecast_result(cached: false)
88	
89	    stub_method(ForecastService, :call, ->(_addr) { forecast }) do
90	      get forecast_path, params: { address: "Chicago, IL" }
91	      assert_select "input[name='address']"
92	      assert_select "input[type='submit']"
93	    end
94	  end
95	
96	  test "blank address redirects back with helpful error message" do
97	    get forecast_path, params: { address: "" }
98	    assert_redirected_to root_path
99	
100	    follow_redirect!
101	    assert_match(/Please enter an address/, response.body)
102	  end
103	
104	  test "accessibility: pages include proper labels and semantic structure" do

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env ruby
2	require "fileutils"
3	
4	APP_ROOT = File.expand_path("..", __dir__)
5	
6	def system!(*args)
7	  system(*args, exception: true)
8	end
9	
10	FileUtils.chdir APP_ROOT do
11	  # This script is a way to set up or update your development environment automatically.
12	  # This script is idempotent, so that you can run it at any time and get an expectable outcome.
13	  # Add necessary setup steps to this file.
14	
15	  puts "== Installing dependencies =="
16	  system("bundle check") || system!("bundle install")
17	
18	  puts "\n== Removing old logs and tempfiles =="
19	  system! "bin/rails log:clear tmp:clear"
20	
21	  unless ARGV.include?("--skip-server")
22	    puts "\n== Starting development server =="
23	    STDOUT.flush # flush the output before exec(2) so that it displays
24	    exec "bin/dev"
25	  end
26	end
27
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-04-14 00:50:17Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./bin/dev`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env sh
2	
3	if ! gem list foreman -i --silent; then
4	  echo "Installing foreman..."
5	  gem install foreman
6	fi
7	
8	# Default to port 3000 if not specified
9	export PORT="${PORT:-3000}"
10	
11	# Let the debug gem allow remote connections,
12	# but avoid loading until `debugger` is called
13	export RUBY_DEBUG_OPEN="true"
14	export RUBY_DEBUG_LAZY="true"
15	
16	exec foreman start -f Procfile.dev "$@"
17
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	<div class="min-h-screen bg-gradient-to-b <%= gradient_class_for(@forecast.current.weather_code) %> px-4 pb-10 pt-6">
2	  <div class="max-w-md mx-auto">
3	
4	    <%# Search bar — allows searching again without going back %>
5	    <div class="bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl border border-white/20 rounded-2xl p-1.5 mb-6">
6	      <%= form_with url: forecast_path, method: :get, class: "flex items-center gap-2" do |f| %>
7	        <div class="flex-1 relative">
8	          <label for="address_show" class="sr-only">City, address, or zip code</label>
9	          <svg class="absolute left-3.5 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-white/40" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">
10	            <path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M21 21l-6-6m2-5a7 7 0 11-14 0 7 7 0 0114 0z"/>
11	          </svg>
12	          <%= f.text_field :address,
13	              id: "address_show",
14	              placeholder: "City, address, or zip code",
15	              value: @address_query,
16	              class: "w-full bg-transparent text-white placeholder-white/40 text-sm font-light pl-10 pr-4 py-3 focus:outline-none",
17	              autocomplete: "street-address" %>
18	        </div>
19	        <%= f.submit "Search",
20	            class: "bg-white/20 hover:bg-white/30 active:bg-white/15 text-white font-medium text-xs px-4 py-2.5 rounded-xl cursor-pointer transition-all duration-200" %>
21	      <% end %>
22	    </div>
23	
24	    <%# Cache indicator — amber dot for cached, green dot for fresh data %>
25	    <% if @forecast.cached? %>
26	      <div class="flex items-center justify-center gap-1.5 mb-4" role="status">
27	        <div class="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-amber-300/80"></div>
28	        <span class="text-white/50 text-xs font-light">
29	          Cached result from <%= @forecast.cached_at&.strftime("%-I:%M %p") || "recently" %>
30	        </span>
31	      </div>
32	    <% else %>
33	      <div class="flex items-center justify-center gap-1.5 mb-4" role="status">
34	        <div class="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-green-300/80"></div>
35	        <span class="text-white/50 text-xs font-light">
36	          Just updated
37	        </span>
38	      </div>
39	    <% end %>
40	
41	    <%# Hero: Location + Current Temperature %>
42	    <section class="text-center mb-8" aria-label="Current conditions">
43	      <h1 class="text-2xl sm:text-3xl font-light text-white tracking-tight mb-1">
44	        <%= @forecast.address %>
45	      </h1>
46	      <p class="text-white/50 text-xs font-light mb-6">ZIP <%= @forecast.zip_code %></p>
47	
48	      <div class="text-7xl sm:text-8xl font-thin text-white leading-none tracking-tighter">
49	        <%= format_temperature(@forecast.current.temperature_f) %><span class="text-4xl sm:text-5xl font-extralight">&deg;</span>
50	      </div>
51	
52	      <p class="text-xl font-light text-white/90 mt-3">
53	        <span role="img" aria-label="<%= @forecast.current.condition %>"><%= @forecast.current.icon %></span>
54	        <%= @forecast.current.condition %>
55	      </p>
56	
57	      <%# Today's high / low from the first day of the forecast %>
58	      <% if @forecast.daily_forecast.any? %>
59	        <p class="text-white/60 font-light mt-2">
60	          H:<%= format_temperature(@forecast.daily_forecast.first.high_f) %>&deg;
61	          &nbsp;
62	          L:<%= format_temperature(@forecast.daily_forecast.first.low_f) %>&deg;
63	        </p>
64	      <% end %>
65	    </section>
66	
67	    <%# Weather Details Card: feels like, humidity, wind %>
68	    <section class="bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl border border-white/20 rounded-2xl p-5 mb-4 shadow-lg" aria-label="Weather details">
69	      <div class="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4">
70	        <div class="text-center">
71	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Feels Like</p>
72	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><%= format_temperature(@forecast.current.feels_like_f) %>&deg;</p>
73	        </div>
74	        <div class="text-center">
75	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Humidity</p>
76	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><%= @forecast.current.humidity %><span class="text-sm">%</span></p>
77	        </div>
78	        <div class="text-center">
79	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Wind</p>
80	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><%= @forecast.current.wind_mph %> <span class="text-sm">mph</span></p>
81	        </div>
82	      </div>
83	    </section>
84	
85	    <%# 5-Day Extended Forecast %>
86	    <% if @forecast.daily_forecast.any? %>
87	      <section class="bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl border border-white/20 rounded-2xl shadow-lg overflow-hidden" aria-label="5-day forecast">
88	        <div class="px-5 pt-4 pb-2">
89	          <h2 class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider">5-Day Forecast</h2>
90	        </div>
91	
92	        <% @forecast.daily_forecast.each_with_index do |day, index| %>
93	          <div class="flex items-center px-5 py-3 <%= 'border-t border-white/10' unless index == 0 %>">
94	            <%# Day name %>
95	            <div class="w-20">
96	              <p class="text-white font-medium text-sm">
97	                <%= index == 0 ? "Today" : day.date.strftime("%A") %>
98	              </p>
99	            </div>
100	
101	            <%# Rain chance — only shown if > 0% %>
102	            <div class="w-14 flex items-center gap-1">
103	              <% if day.chance_of_rain.to_i > 0 %>
104	                <svg class="w-3 h-3 text-blue-200/70" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">
105	                  <path d="M12 2.69l5.66 5.66a8 8 0 1 1-11.31 0z"/>
106	                </svg>
107	                <span class="text-blue-200/70 text-xs font-light"><%= day.chance_of_rain %>%</span>
108	              <% end %>
109	            </div>
110	
111	            <%# Weather icon %>
112	            <div class="flex-1 text-center text-xl">
113	              <span role="img" aria-label="<%= day.condition %>"><%= day.icon %></span>
114	            </div>
115	
116	            <%# High / Low temperatures %>
117	            <div class="flex items-center gap-3 text-right">
118	              <span class="text-white font-light text-sm w-8"><%= format_temperature(day.high_f) %>&deg;</span>
119	              <span class="text-white/40 font-light text-sm w-8"><%= format_temperature(day.low_f) %>&deg;</span>
120	            </div>
121	          </div>
122	        <% end %>
123	      </section>
124	    <% end %>
125	
126	    <%# Footer %>
127	    <p class="text-center text-white/20 text-xs font-light mt-6">
128	      Powered by Open-Meteo &amp; OpenStreetMap
129	    </p>
130	  </div>
131	</div>

## COMPREHENSIVE PRODUCTION READINESS AUDIT: forecastdemo

**Date of Audit:** April 13, 2026  
**Total Lines of Code (app + test):** 1,166

## 1. APPLICATION STRUCTURE INVENTORY

### 1.1 Models (`/app/models/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **weather_code.rb** (56 lines) | WMO weather code mapping (0-99) with descriptions, emojis, and Tailwind CSS gradients | ✅ Excellent |
| **current_weather.rb** (19 lines) | Immutable value object for current conditions | ✅ Good |
| **day_forecast.rb** (17 lines) | Immutable value object for single day forecast | ✅ Good |
| **forecast_result.rb** (23 lines) | Wrapper object combining location, weather, cache metadata | ✅ Good |

**Issues Found in Models:** None

---

### 1.2 Services (`/app/services/`)

| File | Purpose | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| **forecast_service.rb** (52 lines) | Orchestrates geocoding → cache check → weather fetch → cache store workflow | ⚠️ See Issues |
| **weather_service.rb** (115 lines) | Calls Open-Meteo API, parses JSON, builds weather objects | ⚠️ See Issues |
| **geocoding_service.rb** (49 lines) | Converts addresses to coordinates/zip codes via Nominatim; implements reverse-geocode fallback | ⚠️ See Issues |

**Issues Found in Services:**

1. **geocoding_service.rb:46** - **Nil Safety Issue**
   - Line: `reverse&.postal_code.presence`
   - **Problem:** Safe navigation operator `&.` correctly prevents errors, but the `.presence` call could return empty string before `.presence` converts it to `nil`. Pattern is correct but could be clearer.
   - **Severity:** Low - Working as intended
   - **Recommendation:** No change needed; code is defensive.

2. **weather_service.rb:74** - **Type Coercion**
   - Line: `code = data["weather_code"].to_i`
   - **Problem:** Assumes `data["weather_code"]` exists. If missing, `.to_i` on nil → 0 (silent failure).
   - **Severity:** Low - API contract should guarantee this field exists
   - **Recommendation:** Add explicit nil check with error handling OR document API contract.

3. **weather_service.rb:89** - **Defensive Nil Check**
   - Line: `return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?`
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Properly defensive

4. **weather_service.rb:103** - **Nil Coalescing with Default**
   - Line: `chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0`
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Defensively defaults to 0

5. **forecast_service.rb:24** - **Hash Access**
   - Line: `zip_code = location[:zip_code]`
   - **Problem:** Assumes GeocodingService always returns this key. Not checked for nil.
   - **Severity:** Low - Service contract is clear
   - **Recommendation:** Add assertion/validation if needed

---

### 1.3 Controllers (`/app/controllers/`)

|------|---------|--------|
| **application_controller.rb** (4 lines) | Base controller with importmap cache invalidation | ✅ Good |
| **forecasts_controller.rb** (23 lines) | Two actions: index (show form), show (fetch & display forecast) | ⚠️ See Issues |

**Issues Found in Controllers:**

1. **forecasts_controller.rb:8-21** - **Error Handling**
   - **Problem:** `show` action rescues two specific errors but silently swallows all other exceptions
   - **Severity:** Medium - Unhandled exceptions won't be caught
   - **Recommendation:** Add catch-all for unexpected errors OR rely on Rails error middleware (acceptable for this app)
   - **Current Pattern:** ✅ Acceptable for single-page app; all expected errors are handled

2. **forecasts_controller.rb:8** - **Nil Safety**
   - Line: `address = params[:address]`
   - **Problem:** Could be nil, but line 10 checks `.blank?` immediately
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Check happens before use

3. **forecasts_controller.rb:15-16** - **Instance Variable Assignment**
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Both `@forecast` and `@address_query` set for view

## 9. CRITICAL PRODUCTION ISSUES

### 🔴 ISSUE #1: Multi-Server Cache Storage (HIGH PRIORITY)

**Severity:** HIGH  
**Location:** `/Users/kevinfang/forecastdemo/config/environments/production.rb:48`

```ruby
config.cache_store = :memory_store
```

**Impact:**
- Forecast caching (30 minutes TTL) uses in-memory store
- In multi-server deployment, each instance maintains separate cache
- Different servers will make redundant API calls to Open-Meteo/Nominatim
- Increases latency and API rate-limit exposure

**Fix:**
Replace with Redis or Memcached for multi-server:
```ruby
config.cache_store = :redis_cache_store, { url: ENV["REDIS_URL"] }
# OR
config.cache_store = :mem_cache_store, ENV["MEMCACHIER_SERVERS"].split(',')
```

**Single-Server Status:** ✅ Acceptable for now (add comment for future)

---

## 10. SECURITY AUDIT

### ✅ Passed Security Checks:

1. **CSRF Protection:** ✅ Rails CSRF tokens in forms
2. **XSS Prevention:** ✅ ERB auto-escapes by default; tested in `forecasts_controller_test.rb:111-120`
3. **SQL Injection:** ✅ N/A - No database (Rails 8.1 with Active Model only, not Active Record)
4. **SSL:** ✅ `force_ssl = true` in production
5. **HTTP Headers:** ✅ CSP meta tag present
6. **Secure Cookies:** ✅ Cookies sent only over HTTPS in production
7. **User Input Validation:** ✅ Address input validated (not blank)
8. **Sensitive Data Logging:** ✅ `.strip` removes whitespace from user input
9. **API Key Exposure:** ✅ No API keys stored (Open-Meteo and Nominatim are key-free)

### 🟡 Minor Security Considerations:

1. **Timeout Handling:** HTTParty has 10-second timeout; Nominatim has 10-second timeout
   - **Status:** ✅ Good - Prevents hanging requests

2. **Error Messages:** Some error messages expose internal state (e.g., "HTTP 500")
   - **Status:** ✅ Acceptable - Low sensitivity

## 20. SUMMARY & RECOMMENDATIONS

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PRODUCTION READY (Single-Server)

**Strengths:**
1. ✅ Clean, focused architecture with clear separation of concerns
2. ✅ Comprehensive test suite (56 tests, ~2:1 test-to-code ratio)
3. ✅ Excellent error handling with user-friendly messages
4. ✅ Strong security posture (CSRF, XSS, SSL/TLS protection)
5. ✅ Modern Rails 8.1.2 with up-to-date dependencies
6. ✅ Defensive nil handling throughout codebase
7. ✅ Well-documented code with comments explaining design decisions
8. ✅ Proper use of value objects (CurrentWeather, DayForecast, ForecastResult)
9. ✅ Intelligent caching strategy (30-min forecast cache, 1-day geocoding cache)
10. ✅ Accessible UI with proper semantic HTML and ARIA labels

**Weaknesses & Action Items:**

| Priority | Item | Action | File |
|----------|------|--------|------|
| 🔴 HIGH | Multi-server cache store | Use Redis/Memcached if scaling | `config/environments/production.rb:48` |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | README.md | Document setup, deployment, caching | `README.md` |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | Structured logging | Add Rails.logger calls for observability | `app/services/*` |
| 🟢 LOW | Rate limiting | Monitor API usage; add if needed | `app/services/weather_service.rb`, `geocoding_service.rb` |
| 🟢 LOW | Monitoring/Metrics | Set up StatsD/APM in production | (Infrastructure) |

---

## FINAL VERDICT

**This application is PRODUCTION READY for single-server deployments.** The code is well-architected, thoroughly tested, and handles errors gracefully. The only critical change required for multi-server deployments is switching the cache store from memory to Redis or Memcached.

**Deploy with confidence, with these notes:**
1. Use single-server deployment OR configure Redis/Memcached
2. Update README with deployment instructions
3. Monitor cache hit rates and API response times in production
4. Add structured logging for better observability

 `./app/services/geocoding_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Converts a free-form address string into structured location data
4	# using the Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) geocoding service via the geocoder gem.
5	#
6	# Returns a hash with :zip_code, :latitude, :longitude, and :formatted_address.
7	# Raises GeocodingError if the address cannot be resolved or lacks a postal code.
8	class GeocodingService
9	  class GeocodingError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  def self.call(address)
12	    new(address).call
13	  end
14	
15	  def initialize(address)
16	    @address = address.to_s.strip
17	  end
18	
19	  def call
20	    results = Geocoder.search(@address)
21	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not find location for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'" if results.empty?
22	
23	    result = results.first
24	    zip_code = extract_zip_code(result)
25	
26	    # Zip code is required because it serves as the cache key for forecast data.
27	    raise GeocodingError, "Could not determine zip code for '#{@address.truncate(100)}'. Try a more specific address." if zip_code.blank?
28	
29	    {
30	      zip_code: zip_code,
31	      latitude: result.coordinates[0],
32	      longitude: result.coordinates[1],
33	      formatted_address: result.address
34	    }
35	  end
36	
37	  private
38	
39	  # Nominatim omits postal codes for city-level queries. When that happens,
40	  # reverse-geocode the coordinates to get a zip code for the city center.
41	  def extract_zip_code(result)
42	    return result.postal_code if result.postal_code.present?
43	
44	    lat, lon = result.coordinates
45	    reverse = Geocoder.search("#{lat},#{lon}").first
46	    reverse&.postal_code.presence
47	  end
48	end
49
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/forecast_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Orchestrates the full forecast workflow: geocode -> cache check -> fetch -> cache store.
4	#
5	# Caching strategy:
6	# - Cache key is based on zip code (e.g., "forecast_v1/60601")
7	# - TTL is 30 minutes per the requirements
8	# - Uses Rails.cache.read/write (not .fetch) so we can distinguish cache hits from misses
9	#   and pass the `cached` flag + `cached_at` timestamp to the view layer
10	class ForecastService
11	  CACHE_EXPIRY = 30.minutes
12	  CACHE_PREFIX = "forecast_v1"
13	
14	  def self.call(address)
15	    new(address).call
16	  end
17	
18	  def initialize(address)
19	    @address = address
20	  end
21	
22	  def call
23	    location = GeocodingService.call(@address)
24	    zip_code = location[:zip_code]
25	    cache_key = "#{CACHE_PREFIX}/#{zip_code}"
26	
27	    cached_data = Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
28	
29	    if cached_data
30	      build_result(location, cached_data[:weather], zip_code, cached: true, cached_at: cached_data[:fetched_at])
31	    else
32	      weather = WeatherService.call(latitude: location[:latitude], longitude: location[:longitude])
33	      fetched_at = Time.current
34	      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, { weather: weather, fetched_at: fetched_at }, expires_in: CACHE_EXPIRY)
35	      build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached: false, cached_at: fetched_at)
36	    end
37	  end
38	
39	  private
40	
41	  def build_result(location, weather, zip_code, cached:, cached_at:)
42	    ForecastResult.new(
43	      address: location[:formatted_address],
44	      zip_code: zip_code,
45	      current: weather[:current],
46	      daily_forecast: weather[:daily_forecast],
47	      cached: cached,
48	      cached_at: cached_at
49	    )
50	  end
51	end
52
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/weather_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Fetches weather data from the Open-Meteo API (https://open-meteo.com).
4	# No API key required. Returns current conditions and a multi-day forecast.
5	#
6	# Open-Meteo uses WMO weather interpretation codes (mapped by WeatherCode).
7	# API docs: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs
8	class WeatherService
9	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
12	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
13	
14	  # Current weather fields requested from Open-Meteo
15	  CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
16	
17	  # Daily forecast fields requested from Open-Meteo
18	  DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
19	
20	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
21	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
22	  end
23	
24	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
25	    @latitude = latitude
26	    @longitude = longitude
27	  end
28	
29	  def call
30	    response = fetch_forecast
31	    validate_response!(response)
32	    parse_response(response)
33	  end
34	
35	  private
36	
37	  def fetch_forecast
38	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, {
39	      query: {
40	        latitude: @latitude,
41	        longitude: @longitude,
42	        current: CURRENT_PARAMS,
43	        daily: DAILY_PARAMS,
44	        temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
45	        wind_speed_unit: "mph",
46	        forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
47	        timezone: "auto"
48	      },
49	      timeout: 10 # seconds — Open-Meteo can be slow under load
50	    })
51	  end
52	
53	  def validate_response!(response)
54	    return if response.success?
55	
56	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Weather service error (HTTP #{response.code})"
57	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
58	  end
59	
60	  def parse_response(response)
61	    current = build_current_weather(response["current"])
62	    raise WeatherApiError, "Weather data unavailable — no current conditions returned" if current.nil?
63	
64	    {
65	      current: current,
66	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
67	    }
68	  end
69	
70	  def build_current_weather(data)
71	    return nil if data.nil?
72	
73	    # Fetch the full WMO code entry once to avoid repeated hash lookups
74	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
75	    weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
76	
77	    CurrentWeather.new(
78	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
79	      condition: weather_info[:description],
80	      icon: weather_info[:emoji],
81	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
82	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
83	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
84	      weather_code: code
85	    )
86	  end
87	
88	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
89	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
90	
91	    dates = data["time"]
92	    dates.each_index.map do |i|
93	      # Fetch the full WMO code entry once per day
94	      code = data.dig("weather_code", i).to_i
95	      weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
96	
97	      DayForecast.new(
98	        date: parse_date(dates[i]),
99	        high_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_max", i),
100	        low_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_min", i),
101	        condition: weather_info[:description],
102	        icon: weather_info[:emoji],
103	        chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0
104	      )
105	    end
106	  end
107	
108	  # Safely parse a date string, falling back to today if malformed.
109	  def parse_date(date_str)
110	    Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
111	  rescue Date::Error
112	    Date.current
113	  end
114	end
115
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# frozen_string_literal: true

9	class WeatherCode
10	  CODES = {
11	    0  => { description: "Clear Sky",        emoji: "☀️",   gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
12	    1  => { description: "Mainly Clear",     emoji: "🌤️",  gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },

18	    53 => { description: "Moderate Drizzle", emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
19	    55 => { description: "Dense Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-slate-500 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
20	    61 => { description: "Slight Rain",      emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-600 via-slate-600 to-gray-600" },
21	    63 => { description: "Moderate Rain",    emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },

23	    71 => { description: "Slight Snow",      emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-200 via-slate-300 to-gray-400" },
24	    73 => { description: "Moderate Snow",    emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },

28	    81 => { description: "Moderate Showers", emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },

35	  }.each_value(&:freeze).freeze
36	
37	  DEFAULT = { description: "Unknown", emoji: "🌡️", gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-blue-600" }.freeze
38	
39	  # Returns the full info hash { description:, emoji:, gradient: } for a WMO code.
40	  def self.for(code)
41	    CODES.fetch(code, DEFAULT)
42	  end
43

3	module ForecastsHelper
4	  # Returns the Tailwind gradient classes for a given WMO weather code.
5	  # Used to set the page background based on current conditions.
6	  def gradient_class_for(weather_code)
7	    WeatherCode.gradient(weather_code)
8	  end
9	
10	  # Formats a temperature value for display, rounding to the nearest integer.
11	  # Returns "--" if the value is nil (defensive against missing API data).
12	  def format_temperature(temp)
13	    temp ? temp.round.to_s : "--"
14	  end
15	end

3	# Configure the geocoder gem to use Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) for address lookup.
4	# Nominatim is free and requires no API key, but requires a descriptive User-Agent.
5	# See: https://nominatim.org/release-docs/develop/api/Search/
6	Geocoder.configure(
7	  lookup: :nominatim,
8	  language: :en,
9	  use_https: true,
10	  http_headers: {
11	    "User-Agent" => "ForecastDemo/1.0 (Rails weather forecast demo app)"
12	  },
13	  timeout: 10, # seconds — Nominatim can be slow under heavy load
14	  cache: Rails.cache,
15	  cache_options: {
16	    expiration: 1.day, # Geocoding results rarely change; cache aggressively
17	    prefix: "geocoder:"
18	  }
19	)

7	  # Settings specified here will take precedence over those in config/application.rb.
8	
9	  # While tests run files are not watched, reloading is not necessary.
10	  config.enable_reloading = false
11	
12	  # Eager loading loads your entire application. When running a single test locally,
13	  # this is usually not necessary, and can slow down your test suite. However, it's
14	  # recommended that you enable it in continuous integration systems to ensure eager
15	  # loading is working properly before deploying your code.
16	  config.eager_load = ENV["CI"].present?
17	
18	  # Configure public file server for tests with cache-control for performance.
19	  config.public_file_server.headers = { "cache-control" => "public, max-age=3600" }
20

30

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:52:19Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now let me read the remaining files: views, test files, layout, controller tests, etc.

Key issues I've identified so far:

1. **Network timeout not rescued in controller** — If HTTParty raises a timeout error (e.g., `Net::OpenTimeout`, `Net::ReadTimeout`, `Errno::ECONNREFUSED`), the controller only rescues `GeocodingError` and `WeatherApiError`. Network errors will crash with a 500 error.

2. **Geocoder errors not rescued in controller** — If `Geocoder.search` itself raises a network error (not our custom error), it won't be caught.


3. **Unhandled exceptions from both libraries** — HTTParty and Geocoder can raise `Net::OpenTimeout`, `Net::ReadTimeout`, `Errno::ECONNREFUSED`, `SocketError`, `Geocoder::LookupTimeout`, `Geocoder::NetworkError`, and `Geocoder::Error`, none of which are subclasses of our custom errors, so they'll bubble up as 500 errors instead of being gracefully handled.

4. **Solution** — I should add rescue blocks in the controller to catch these network-related exceptions and return friendly error messages, since both services can throw them. This is cleaner than wrapping each call individually in the services.

5. **Next step** — I need to review the test files to ensure they cover these error scenarios comprehensively.
</details></think>

1	# frozen_string_literal: true

3	require "test_helper"
4	
5	class WeatherServiceTest < ActiveSupport::TestCase
6	  test "parses a successful API response into structured weather data" do
7	    response = mock_http_response(200, build_mock_open_meteo_response)
8	
9	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
10	      result = WeatherService.call(latitude: 41.8781, longitude: -87.6298)
11	
12	      # Current weather
13	      assert_instance_of CurrentWeather, result[:current]
14	      assert_equal 72.0, result[:current].temperature_f
15	      assert_equal "Partly Cloudy", result[:current].condition
16	      assert_equal "⛅", result[:current].icon
17	      assert_equal 45, result[:current].humidity
18	      assert_equal 8.5, result[:current].wind_mph
19	      assert_equal 70.0, result[:current].feels_like_f
20	      assert_equal 2, result[:current].weather_code
21	
22	      # Daily forecast
23	      assert_equal 3, result[:daily_forecast].length
24	      first_day = result[:daily_forecast].first
25	      assert_instance_of DayForecast, first_day
26	      assert_equal Date.today, first_day.date
27	      assert_equal 78.0, first_day.high_f
28	      assert_equal 62.0, first_day.low_f
29	      assert_equal "Partly Cloudy", first_day.condition
30	      assert_equal 10, first_day.chance_of_rain
31	    end
32	  end
33	
34	  test "raises WeatherApiError on non-success HTTP response" do
35	    response = mock_http_response(400, { "reason" => "Bad request" })
36	
37	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do
38	      error = assert_raises(WeatherService::WeatherApiError) do
39	        WeatherService.call(latitude: 0, longitude: 0)
40	      end
41	      assert_includes error.message, "Bad request"
42	    end
43	  end
44	
45	  test "provides fallback error message when API reason is missing" do
46	    response = mock_http_response(500, {})
47	
48	    stub_method(HTTParty, :get, ->(*_args, **_kwargs) { response }) do

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 00:52:54Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me identify the issues that need fixing:

## Critical Issues

### 1. Network errors not handled in the controller
The controller only rescues `GeocodingService::GeocodingError` and `WeatherService::WeatherApiError`. But network errors from HTTParty and Geocoder (timeouts, connection refused, DNS failures) will bubble up as unhandled exceptions, causing a 500 error page.

Errors that can occur:
- `Net::OpenTimeout` — connection timeout (from HTTParty)
- `Net::ReadTimeout` — read timeout (from HTTParty)


- `Errno::ECONNREFUSED` — connection refused
- `SocketError` — DNS failure
- `Geocoder::LookupTimeout` — geocoder timeout
- `Geocoder::NetworkError` — geocoder network error

I need to add a catch-all for these network errors in the controller and show a user-friendly message.

### 2. The `hello_controller.js` is dead code
The Stimulus `hello_controller.js` is a Rails scaffold default that's not used. Not a bug, but it's clutter.

### 3. `config/environments/development.rb` — I should check if it uses caching properly

Actually, let me focus on the real bugs and gaps instead of cosmetic cleanup. The main issue is that network errors from the geocoding or weather services will crash with a 500 error instead of gracefully handling the failure. I should wrap those calls in rescue blocks to catch timeouts and connection failures, then display a friendly message to the user. I also need to write tests covering these error scenarios, plus some edge cases like very long addresses, special characters, international addresses, and inputs that geocode but have no weather data available. Once I fix the error handling and add the tests, I'll do some live testing to make sure everything works.

Now I'll implement the fixes and write the README.
</details></think>

# Edge case 1: Homepage loads
echo "=== Test 1: Homepage ==="

# Edge case 2: Blank address
echo -e "\n=== Test 2: Blank address ==="
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://localhost:3000/forecast?address=")
echo "Status: $HTTP_CODE (expect 302)"

# Edge case 3: Whitespace-only address
echo -e "\n=== Test 3: Whitespace-only address ==="
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://localhost:3000/forecast?address=%20%20%20")
echo "Status: $HTTP_CODE (expect 302)"

# Edge case 4: No address param at all
echo -e "\n=== Test 4: Missing address param ==="
HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "http://localhost:3000/forecast")
echo "Status: $HTTP_CODE (expect 302)"
```

echo "Status: $HTTP_CODE (expect 302 redirect)"

```md
# Weather Forecast

A Ruby on Rails weather forecast application with an Apple Weather-inspired design. Enter any address, city, or zip code to view current conditions and a 5-day extended forecast with real-time data.

## Features

- **Current conditions** -- temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed, and weather description with emoji icons
- **5-day extended forecast** -- daily high/low temperatures, weather conditions, and precipitation probability
- **30-minute caching by zip code** -- subsequent requests for the same zip code are served from cache, with a visual indicator distinguishing fresh vs. cached results
- **Reverse-geocode fallback** -- city-level queries that lack a postal code automatically resolve via reverse geocoding
- **Apple Weather-inspired UI** -- glass morphism cards, weather-contextual gradient backgrounds, system font stack, responsive typography
- **Accessible** -- semantic HTML, ARIA labels, screen-reader-only labels, `role="status"` for cache indicators
- **No API keys required** -- uses Open-Meteo (weather) and Nominatim/OpenStreetMap (geocoding), both free and keyless
- **No database** -- fully stateless, service-based architecture

## Screenshots

| Home | Forecast |
|------|----------|
| Search screen with glass morphism card | Weather display with contextual gradient background |

## Tech Stack

| Component | Technology |
|-----------|-----------|
| Framework | Ruby 3.4.5, Rails 8.1 |
| Weather API | [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com) (free, no key) |
| Geocoding | [Nominatim](https://nominatim.org) via `geocoder` gem (free, no key) |
| HTTP Client | `httparty` |
| CSS | Tailwind CSS v4 via `tailwindcss-rails` (standalone binary, no Node.js) |
| Frontend | Hotwire (Turbo Drive), Stimulus |
| Asset Pipeline | Propshaft, importmap-rails |
| Server | Puma |
| Caching | `Rails.cache` with `:memory_store` (30-min TTL by zip code) |

## Architecture

\```
User enters address
  -> ForecastsController#show
    -> ForecastService (orchestrator)
      -> GeocodingService (Nominatim) -> zip_code + lat/lon
      -> Check Rails.cache["forecast_v1/{zip_code}"]
        -> HIT:  return cached data (cached=true, cached_at timestamp)
        -> MISS: WeatherService (Open-Meteo) -> cache 30 min -> return fresh
    -> Render show.html.erb with ForecastResult
\```

### Key Design Decisions

- **Cache key is zip code** -- all addresses within the same zip code share a single cache entry, reducing redundant API calls
- **`Rails.cache.read`/`write` instead of `.fetch`** -- allows distinguishing cache hits from misses so the view can show the correct indicator
- **Service object pattern** -- each service has a single responsibility: `GeocodingService` (address to coordinates), `WeatherService` (coordinates to weather), `ForecastService` (orchestration + caching)
- **PORO value objects** -- `ForecastResult`, `CurrentWeather`, `DayForecast`, `WeatherCode` are plain Ruby objects with no framework dependencies
- **WMO weather codes** -- Open-Meteo uses WMO standard codes; `WeatherCode` maps each to a description, emoji, and Tailwind gradient class

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Ruby 3.4.5 (see `.ruby-version`)
- Bundler

### Setup

\```bash
git clone <repo-url> && cd forecastdemo
bin/setup
\```

This installs gems and prepares the environment. No database setup is needed.

### Run the App

\```bash
bin/dev
\```

This starts both the Rails server and the Tailwind CSS watcher via Foreman. Visit [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).

Alternatively, build CSS once and start the server directly:

\```bash
bin/rails tailwindcss:build
bin/rails server
\```

### Run Tests

\```bash
bin/rails test
\```

Runs the full test suite: 58 tests, 317 assertions. All tests use stubs for external API calls -- no network access required.

### Linting and Security

\```bash
bin/rubocop          # Ruby style checks (RuboCop + Rails Omakase)
bin/brakeman         # Static security analysis
bin/bundler-audit    # Gem vulnerability audit
bin/ci               # Runs all of the above + tests
\```

## Project Structure

\```
app/
  models/
    weather_code.rb        # WMO code -> description, emoji, gradient mapping
    current_weather.rb     # Value object: current conditions
    day_forecast.rb        # Value object: single day forecast
    forecast_result.rb     # Value object: complete forecast + cache metadata
  services/
    geocoding_service.rb   # Address -> {zip_code, lat, lon} via Nominatim
    weather_service.rb     # Lat/lon -> weather data via Open-Meteo API
    forecast_service.rb    # Orchestrator: geocode -> cache -> fetch -> result
  controllers/
    forecasts_controller.rb  # index (search form) + show (results)
  helpers/
    forecasts_helper.rb    # gradient_class_for(), format_temperature()
  views/
    forecasts/
      index.html.erb       # Search screen with glass morphism card
      show.html.erb        # Weather display with contextual gradient
    layouts/
      application.html.erb # Base layout with Apple system font stack
  assets/
    tailwind/
      application.css      # Tailwind v4 config with @source for model scanning

test/
  models/                  # WeatherCode mapping, ForecastResult defaults
  services/                # Geocoding, Weather API, cache behavior
  controllers/             # Routes, error handling, XSS, network errors
  integration/             # Full user flows end-to-end

config/
  initializers/
    geocoder.rb            # Nominatim config with User-Agent and 1-day cache
  routes.rb                # root + GET /forecast
\```

## Caching Behavior

| Cache Layer | TTL | Key | Purpose |
|-------------|-----|-----|---------|
| Forecast data | 30 minutes | `forecast_v1/{zip_code}` | Avoid redundant weather API calls |
| Geocoding results | 1 day | `geocoder:{address}` | Avoid redundant geocoding lookups |

The cache indicator on the forecast page shows:
- **Green dot + "Just updated"** -- fresh data from the API
- **Amber dot + "Cached result from [time]"** -- served from cache

For single-server deployments, the default `:memory_store` works well. For multi-server production, switch to Redis or Memcached in `config/environments/production.rb`:

\```ruby
config.cache_store = :redis_cache_store, { url: ENV["REDIS_URL"] }
\```

## Error Handling

| Scenario | User Experience |
|----------|----------------|
| Blank/missing address | Redirect to home with "Please enter an address" |
| Address not found | Redirect with "Could not find location for '...'" |
| No zip code resolvable | Redirect with "Could not determine zip code... Try a more specific address" |
| Weather API error | Redirect with "Weather data unavailable: ..." |
| Network timeout / DNS failure | Redirect with "Unable to reach weather services. Please try again" |

All errors are shown as styled flash alerts on the search screen. No raw error pages are exposed to users.

## Testing

The test suite covers:

- **Unit tests** -- WeatherCode mappings, ForecastResult value object, nil defaults
- **Service tests** -- Geocoding (including reverse-geocode fallback), Weather API parsing (WMO codes, nil data, unknown codes), ForecastService (cache hit/miss/expiry, zip-based sharing, error propagation)
- **Controller tests** -- All routes, error paths, network errors (timeout, DNS, connection refused), XSS prevention, edge cases (whitespace, missing params)
- **Integration tests** -- Full user flows: search -> results -> cache indicator, error flows, accessibility checks

All external API calls are stubbed using a custom `StubHelper` module compatible with Minitest 6.

## Deployment

The app is ready for single-server deployment. Key production settings (`config/environments/production.rb`):

- SSL forced (`config.force_ssl = true`)
- Eager loading enabled
- Memory cache store (swap to Redis for multi-server)
- Thruster for HTTP caching/compression (`bin/thrust`)

\```bash
RAILS_ENV=production bin/rails tailwindcss:build
RAILS_ENV=production bin/thrust bin/rails server
\```

## License

This project is for demonstration purposes.

All clean. Here's a summary of everything reviewed and fixed:

---

## Review Summary

### Tests: 58 runs, 317 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors

### Issues Found & Fixed

1. **Network errors not handled in controller** (`app/controllers/forecasts_controller.rb`) -- Added rescue for `Net::OpenTimeout`, `Net::ReadTimeout`, `Errno::ECONNREFUSED`, `SocketError`, `Errno::EHOSTUNREACH`, `Errno::ENETUNREACH`. Users now see a friendly "Unable to reach weather services" message instead of a 500 error when APIs are unreachable.

2. **Added 5 new tests** (`test/controllers/forecasts_controller_test.rb`):
   - Network timeout handling
   - DNS resolution failure handling
   - Connection refused handling
   - Whitespace-only address treated as blank
   - Search field preserves the user's query on the results page

### Live Edge Cases Tested (all passing)

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| Homepage loads | 200 |
| Blank address | 302 redirect with flash |
| Whitespace-only address | 302 redirect with flash |
| Missing address param | 302 redirect with flash |
| Chicago, IL | 200 with real weather data, ZIP 60604 |
| Los Angeles, CA | 200, ZIP 90012 |
| Zip code only (90210) | 200 |
| Nonsense address | 302 redirect with geocoding error |
| Special chars (AT&T Park) | 200 |
| Unicode (Zurich) | 200 |
| Very long address (300 chars) | 302 redirect |
| Cache indicator (fresh) | "Just updated" |
| Cache indicator (cached) | "Cached result from [time]" |
| Health check /up | 200 |
| XSS in address | Safely escaped, 0 raw script tags |
| Gradient background rendering | Correct weather-contextual gradient |
| 5-day forecast | 5 days rendered with icons |
| Weather details | Feels Like, Humidity, Wind all present |
| All 14 gradient CSS classes | Compiled in Tailwind output |
| Development log errors | Zero errors, zero 500s |

### README
Comprehensive `README.md` written covering features, architecture, setup, testing, caching, error handling, deployment, and project structure.

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	class ForecastsController < ApplicationController
4	  def index
5	  end
6	
7	  def show
8	    address = params[:address]
9	
10	    if address.blank?
11	      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Please enter an address."
12	      return
13	    end
14	
15	    @forecast = ForecastService.call(address)
16	    @address_query = address
17	  rescue GeocodingService::GeocodingError => e
18	    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
19	  rescue WeatherService::WeatherApiError => e
20	    redirect_to root_path, alert: "Weather data unavailable: #{e.message}"
21	  rescue Net::OpenTimeout, Net::ReadTimeout, Errno::ECONNREFUSED, SocketError,
22	         Errno::EHOSTUNREACH, Errno::ENETUNREACH => e
23	    Rails.logger.error("[ForecastsController] Network error: #{e.class} - #{e.message}")
24	    redirect_to root_path, alert: "Unable to reach weather services. Please try again in a moment."
25	  end
26	end
27
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 01:13:30Z)**_

**Feature 1: Stimulus controller for unit toggle (°F/°C)**

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 01:13:39Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus"

// Toggles all temperature displays between °F and °C, and wind between mph and km/h.
// Persists the user's preference in localStorage across sessions.
export default class extends Controller {
  static targets = ["temp", "wind", "windUnit", "btnF", "btnC"]

  connect() {
    this.unit = localStorage.getItem("weatherUnit") || "F"
    this.apply()
  }

  toggle(event) {
    this.unit = event.params.unit
    localStorage.setItem("weatherUnit", this.unit)
    this.apply()
  }

  apply() {
    const isMetric = this.unit === "C"

    this.tempTargets.forEach(el => {
      const f = parseFloat(el.dataset.tempF)
      if (isNaN(f)) return
      el.textContent = isMetric ? Math.round((f - 32) * 5 / 9) : Math.round(f)
    })

    this.windTargets.forEach(el => {
      const mph = parseFloat(el.dataset.windMph)
      if (isNaN(mph)) return
      el.textContent = isMetric ? (mph * 1.60934).toFixed(1) : mph
    })

    this.windUnitTargets.forEach(el => {
      el.textContent = isMetric ? "km/h" : "mph"
    })

    if (this.hasBtnFTarget && this.hasBtnCTarget) {
      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", !isMetric)
      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", !isMetric)
      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", isMetric)
      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", isMetric)
      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", isMetric)
      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", isMetric)
      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", !isMetric)
      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", !isMetric)
    }
  }
}

```

```
</details></tool-use>

**Feature 4: Stimulus controller for address autocomplete**

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 01:13:56Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus"

// Provides address autocomplete by fetching suggestions from the /autocomplete endpoint.
// Debounces input, supports keyboard navigation (arrows + Enter + Escape), and
// closes on outside clicks.
export default class extends Controller {
  static targets = ["input", "list"]
  static values = { url: String }

  connect() {
    this.selectedIndex = -1
    this.timeout = null
    this._clickOutside = this.clickOutside.bind(this)
    document.addEventListener("click", this._clickOutside)
  }

  disconnect() {
    clearTimeout(this.timeout)
    document.removeEventListener("click", this._clickOutside)
  }

  onInput() {
    clearTimeout(this.timeout)
    const query = this.inputTarget.value.trim()

    if (query.length < 3) {
      this.hideList()
      return
    }

    this.timeout = setTimeout(() => this.search(query), 300)
  }

  async search(query) {
    try {
      const response = await fetch(`${this.urlValue}?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}`)
      if (!response.ok) { this.hideList(); return }
      const suggestions = await response.json()
      this.showSuggestions(suggestions)
    } catch {
      this.hideList()
    }
  }

  showSuggestions(suggestions) {
    if (suggestions.length === 0) {
      this.hideList()
      return
    }

    this.selectedIndex = -1
    this.listTarget.innerHTML = suggestions.map((s, i) =>
      `<li data-index="${i}"
           class="px-4 py-2.5 cursor-pointer text-white/80 text-sm font-light border-b border-white/5 last:border-0 transition-colors"
           role="option">
        ${this.escapeHtml(s.display_name)}
      </li>`
    ).join("")
    this.listTarget.classList.remove("hidden")
  }

  select(event) {
    const li = event.target.closest("li")
    if (!li) return
    const text = li.textContent.trim()
    this.inputTarget.value = text
    this.hideList()
    this.inputTarget.closest("form").requestSubmit()
  }

  onKeydown(event) {
    if (!this.hasListTarget || this.listTarget.classList.contains("hidden")) return

    const items = this.listTarget.querySelectorAll("li")
    if (items.length === 0) return

    switch (event.key) {
      case "ArrowDown":
        event.preventDefault()
        this.selectedIndex = Math.min(this.selectedIndex + 1, items.length - 1)
        this.highlightItem(items)
        break
      case "ArrowUp":
        event.preventDefault()
        this.selectedIndex = Math.max(this.selectedIndex - 1, -1)
        this.highlightItem(items)
        break
      case "Enter":
        if (this.selectedIndex >= 0) {
          event.preventDefault()
          items[this.selectedIndex].click()
        }
        break
      case "Escape":
        this.hideList()
        break
    }
  }

  highlightItem(items) {
    items.forEach((item, i) => {
      if (i === this.selectedIndex) {
        item.classList.add("bg-white/10")
      } else {
        item.classList.remove("bg-white/10")
      }
    })
  }

  hideList() {
    if (this.hasListTarget) {
      this.listTarget.classList.add("hidden")
      this.listTarget.innerHTML = ""
    }
    this.selectedIndex = -1
  }

  clickOutside(event) {
    if (!this.element.contains(event.target)) {
      this.hideList()
    }
  }

  escapeHtml(text) {
    const div = document.createElement("div")
    div.textContent = text
    return div.innerHTML
  }
}

```
