143	              <% if day.chance_of_rain.to_i > 0 %>
144	                <svg class="w-3 h-3 text-blue-200/70 shrink-0" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">
145	                  <path d="M12 2.69l5.66 5.66a8 8 0 1 1-11.31 0z"/>
146	                </svg>
147	                <span class="text-blue-200/70 text-xs font-light"><%= day.chance_of_rain %>%</span>
148	              <% end %>
149	            </div>
150	
151	            <%# Weather condition: icon + description %>
152	            <div class="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center gap-1.5 min-w-0">
153	              <span aria-hidden="true" class="text-lg shrink-0"><%= day.icon %></span>
154	              <span class="text-white/60 text-xs font-light truncate"><%= day.condition %></span>
155	            </div>
156	
157	            <%# High / Low temperatures %>
158	            <div class="w-10 text-right">
159	              <span class="text-white font-light text-sm"><span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= day.high_f %>"><%= format_temperature(day.high_f) %></span>&deg;</span>
160	            </div>
161	            <div class="w-10 text-right">
162	              <span class="text-white/40 font-light text-sm"><span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= day.low_f %>"><%= format_temperature(day.low_f) %></span>&deg;</span>
163	            </div>
164	          </div>
165	        <% end %>
166	      </section>
167	    <% end %>
168	
169	    <%# Footer %>
170	    <p class="text-center text-white/20 text-xs font-light mt-6">
171	      Powered by Open-Meteo &amp; OpenStreetMap
172	    </p>
173	  </div>
174	</div>
175
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me analyze all 5 issues:

1. **Wednesday overlap with rain icon** — The 5-day forecast row layout uses `flex` with fixed widths. The day name column is `w-[4.5rem]` which is ~72px. "Wednesday" is a long word that could overflow. The rain column is `w-12` (48px). These fixed widths can cause overlap when the day name is long. I need to ensure the day name doesn't overflow into the rain column.

2. **Low column on left side of High column** — Currently the order is: Day | Rain | Condition | High | Low. The user wants: Day | Rain | Condition | Low | High. Actually wait, let me re-read: "make the low column on the left side of high column." So Low should come before High. 

I need to swap the High and Low columns.

3. **Displaying current temperature** — Looking at the hero section, yes it does display the current temperature at line 78-79. But let me think... the user is asking "Are you sure you are displaying not only the high and low, but also the current temperature?" - maybe the current temperature display isn't clear enough, or maybe they want current temperature shown in the 5-day forecast section too? Looking at it, the hero shows `@forecast.current.temperature_f` which is the current temp, and below it shows High/Low from the daily forecast. Let me make sure the current temperature is clearly labeled.

4. **Dropdown**

The autocomplete dropdown is too transparent with its current styling — both the dropdown container and the individual list items need more opacity so they're actually readable. I should change the background from semi-transparent white to something more solid, and increase the text opacity as well.

5. **Allow user to go back to the home page** — The show page currently has a search bar but no explicit "back" or "home" link/button. Need to add navigation back to home.

Let me fix all these issues. I'm thinking about the 5-day forecast text overlap problem — I could use abbreviated day names like Mon, Tue, Wed instead of full names, or make the column wider, or adjust the row layout to be more responsive. Let me consider which approach works best.

The issue is that "Wednesday" takes up about 9 characters and needs roughly 80px of space, but the column is only 72px wide. Using abbreviated day names with `%a` format (Mon, Tue, Wed, etc.) would be the cleanest solution and matches how Apple Weather displays it. I'll keep "Today" for the first day and use the 3-letter abbreviations for the rest of the week.

For the other fixes: I should swap the Low and High columns to match the more intuitive Low | High order, and I need to make the current temperature more prominent by adding a label or making it clearer that it's the current reading rather than just a large unlabeled number.

The dropdown also needs to be more opaque—I'll replace the semi-transparent backdrop blur styling with a solid dark background color, and update the list items in the autocomplete controller to match that darker theme.

For the navigation, I should add a way to get back to the home page from the show page, either through a back arrow or by making the app title clickable.

Now I'm going to implement these fixes. For the dropdown, I'll use a solid dark gray background with matching borders, and style the list items with full opacity text and subtle dividers between them. I'm settling on a dark gray dropdown with `bg-gray-900` and `border-gray-600` for contrast against the blue gradient backgrounds, then updating the item styling to use solid backgrounds instead of transparent overlays, and changing the keyboard navigation highlight from the semi-transparent white to a more visible `bg-gray-700`. I'm adding a subtle label above the current temperature to make it clear that the large number represents the current conditions, not just the high and low. For the forecast section, I need to review the column layout to ensure all the necessary information is being displayed clearly. I'm working through a series of edits to clean up the weather display template. I need to reorder the Low and High columns, add a home link and current temperature label, abbreviate day names to prevent text overlap, and update the dropdown styling across multiple files to use solid backgrounds instead of transparent ones.

For the day names, I'm switching from the full format to abbreviated versions like "Mon" and "Tue" so they fit better in the constrained width. I should also add truncate as a safety measure for the condition text since that column has flexible width.

Looking at the column order, I need to swap Low and High so Low appears on the left side of High, which means updating both the headers and the data rows. Now I'm examining the dropdown styling to make it more solid and consistent across the show and index pages.

Updating dropdown styles...

100	          &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp;
101	          Low: <span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= @forecast.daily_forecast.first.low_f %>"><%= format_temperature(@forecast.daily_forecast.first.low_f) %></span>&deg;

103	      <% end %>

106	    <%# Weather Details Card: feels like, humidity, wind %>
107	    <section class="bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl border border-white/20 rounded-2xl p-5 mb-4 shadow-lg" aria-label="Weather details">
108	      <h2 class="text-white/30 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-3">Current Details</h2>
109	      <div class="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4">
110	        <div class="text-center">
111	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Feels Like</p>
112	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= @forecast.current.feels_like_f %>"><%= format_temperature(@forecast.current.feels_like_f) %></span>&deg;</p>
113	        </div>
114	        <div class="text-center">
115	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Humidity</p>
116	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><%= @forecast.current.humidity %><span class="text-sm">%</span></p>
117	        </div>
118	        <div class="text-center">
119	          <p class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider mb-1">Wind</p>
120	          <p class="text-white text-xl font-light"><span data-unit-toggle-target="wind" data-wind-mph="<%= @forecast.current.wind_mph %>"><%= @forecast.current.wind_mph %></span> <span class="text-sm" data-unit-toggle-target="windUnit">mph</span></p>
121	        </div>
122	      </div>
123	    </section>
124	
125	    <%# 5-Day Extended Forecast %>
126	    <% if @forecast.daily_forecast.any? %>
127	      <section class="bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl border border-white/20 rounded-2xl shadow-lg overflow-hidden" aria-label="5-day forecast">
128	        <div class="px-5 pt-4 pb-2">
129	          <h2 class="text-white/40 text-xs font-light uppercase tracking-wider">5-Day Forecast</h2>
130	        </div>
131	
132	        <%# Column headers %>
133	        <div class="flex items-center px-5 py-1.5 text-white/30 text-[10px] font-light uppercase tracking-wider">
134	          <div class="w-14 shrink-0">Day</div>
135	          <div class="w-12 shrink-0">Rain</div>
136	          <div class="flex-1 text-center">Condition</div>
137	          <div class="w-10 text-right shrink-0">Low</div>
138	          <div class="w-10 text-right shrink-0">High</div>
139	        </div>
140	
141	        <% @forecast.daily_forecast.each_with_index do |day, index| %>
142	          <div class="flex items-center px-5 py-3 <%= 'border-t border-white/10' unless index == 0 %>">
143	            <%# Day name (abbreviated to prevent overlap) %>
144	            <div class="w-14 shrink-0">
145	              <p class="text-white font-medium text-sm">
146	                <%= index == 0 ? "Today" : day.date.strftime("%a") %>
147	              </p>
148	            </div>
149	
150	            <%# Rain chance %>
151	            <div class="w-12 shrink-0 flex items-center gap-1">
152	              <% if day.chance_of_rain.to_i > 0 %>
153	                <svg class="w-3 h-3 text-blue-200/70 shrink-0" fill="currentColor" viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">
154	                  <path d="M12 2.69l5.66 5.66a8 8 0 1 1-11.31 0z"/>
155	                </svg>
156	                <span class="text-blue-200/70 text-xs font-light"><%= day.chance_of_rain %>%</span>
157	              <% end %>
158	            </div>
159	
160	            <%# Weather condition: icon + description %>
161	            <div class="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center gap-1.5 min-w-0">
162	              <span aria-hidden="true" class="text-lg shrink-0"><%= day.icon %></span>
163	              <span class="text-white/60 text-xs font-light truncate"><%= day.condition %></span>
164	            </div>
165	
166	            <%# Low / High temperatures (low on left, high on right) %>
167	            <div class="w-10 text-right shrink-0">
168	              <span class="text-white/40 font-light text-sm"><span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= day.low_f %>"><%= format_temperature(day.low_f) %></span>&deg;</span>
169	            </div>
170	            <div class="w-10 text-right shrink-0">
171	              <span class="text-white font-light text-sm"><span data-unit-toggle-target="temp" data-temp-f="<%= day.high_f %>"><%= format_temperature(day.high_f) %></span>&deg;</span>
172	            </div>
173	          </div>
174	        <% end %>
175	      </section>
176	    <% end %>
177	
178	    <%# Footer %>
179	    <p class="text-center text-white/20 text-xs font-light mt-6">
180	      Powered by Open-Meteo &amp; OpenStreetMap
181	    </p>
182	  </div>
183	</div>
184

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-14 01:43:11Z)**_

Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all 5 fixes:

1. **Overlap fix** — Day names in the 5-day forecast now use 3-letter abbreviations (`%a` → Mon, Tue, Wed) instead of full names. All columns have `shrink-0` to prevent flex compression.

2. **Low before High** — Column order is now `Day | Rain | Condition | Low | High` in both headers and data rows.

3. **Current temperature labeled** — Added a "CURRENT TEMPERATURE" label above the hero temperature number so it's clearly distinguished from high/low.

4. **Opaque dropdown** — Autocomplete dropdown changed from `bg-white/10 backdrop-blur-2xl` (transparent) to `bg-gray-900 border-gray-600 shadow-xl` (solid dark background) on both pages. List items also use solid colors with `hover:bg-gray-800`.

5. **Home link** — Added a `< Home` back link at the top of the forecast results page that navigates to the home page.

**82 tests, 436 assertions, 0 failures.** You can verify visually with `bin/dev`.

```text
source "https://rubygems.org"

# Bundle edge Rails instead: gem "rails", github: "rails/rails", branch: "main"
gem "rails", "~> 8.1.2"
# The modern asset pipeline for Rails [https://github.com/rails/propshaft]
gem "propshaft"
# Use the Puma web server [https://github.com/puma/puma]
gem "puma", ">= 5.0"
# Use JavaScript with ESM import maps [https://github.com/rails/importmap-rails]
gem "importmap-rails"
# Hotwire's SPA-like page accelerator [https://turbo.hotwired.dev]
gem "turbo-rails"
# Hotwire's modest JavaScript framework [https://stimulus.hotwired.dev]
gem "stimulus-rails"
# Use Tailwind CSS [https://github.com/rails/tailwindcss-rails]
gem "tailwindcss-rails"
# Build JSON APIs with ease [https://github.com/rails/jbuilder]
gem "jbuilder"
# Geocoding addresses to coordinates and zip codes [https://github.com/alexreisner/geocoder]
gem "geocoder", "~> 1.8"
# HTTP requests to weather APIs [https://github.com/jnunemaker/httparty]
gem "httparty", "~> 0.24"

# Use Active Model has_secure_password [https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_model_basics.html#securepassword]
# gem "bcrypt", "~> 3.1.7"

# Windows does not include zoneinfo files, so bundle the tzinfo-data gem
gem "tzinfo-data", platforms: %i[ windows jruby ]

# Reduces boot times through caching; required in config/boot.rb
gem "bootsnap", require: false

# Add HTTP asset caching/compression and X-Sendfile acceleration to Puma [https://github.com/basecamp/thruster/]
gem "thruster", require: false

group :development, :test do
  # See https://guides.rubyonrails.org/debugging_rails_applications.html#debugging-with-the-debug-gem
  gem "debug", platforms: %i[ mri windows ], require: "debug/prelude"

  # Audits gems for known security defects (use config/bundler-audit.yml to ignore issues)
  gem "bundler-audit", require: false

  # Static analysis for security vulnerabilities [https://brakemanscanner.org/]
  gem "brakeman", require: false

  # Omakase Ruby styling [https://github.com/rails/rubocop-rails-omakase/]
  gem "rubocop-rails-omakase", require: false
end

group :development do
  # Use console on exceptions pages [https://github.com/rails/web-console]
  gem "web-console"
end
```
</details></tool-use>

Perfect! I have successfully read all 31 files from your Rails weather forecast application. Here's the complete audit report:

---

## AUDIT REPORT: Weather Forecast Rails App

### FILE CONTENTS SUMMARY

I've read and reviewed all 31 files you requested. Below is a comprehensive audit organized by category:

---

## 1. CONFIG FILES

### config/application.rb
- Standard Rails 8.1 configuration
- Uses minimal railtie requires (no database, jobs, cable, mailer)
- Sets `config.load_defaults 8.1` correctly
- Ignores `lib/assets` and `lib/tasks` from autoloading

**Code Quality:** Clean, minimal, appropriate

### config/routes.rb
- Simple routing: health check (`/up`), root to `forecasts#index`, `forecast` show action, and autocomplete endpoint
- All routes clearly defined

**Code Quality:** Clean and maintainable

### config/initializers/geocoder.rb
- Nominatim geocoding configured with proper User-Agent
- 10-second timeout appropriate for external API
- Cache enabled with 1-day TTL with `geocoder:` prefix
- Uses `Rails.cache` correctly

**Code Quality:** Well-documented, secure (proper User-Agent handling)

### config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb
- **FINDING:** Entire CSP configuration is commented out
- **Risk:** No CSP protection in production
- **Recommendation:** Enable CSP for security. Should at least have:
  ```ruby
  config.content_security_policy do |policy|
    policy.default_src :self, :https
    policy.script_src :self, :https
    policy.style_src :self, :https
  end
  ```

### config/initializers/inflections.rb
- Completely commented default file
- No custom inflections needed

**Code Quality:** Fine for this app

### config/initializers/filter_parameter_logging.rb
- Properly filters sensitive parameters: `:passw`, `:email`, `:secret`, `:token`, `:_key`, `:crypt`, `:salt`, `:certificate`, `:otp`, `:ssn`, `:cvv`, `:cvc`
- Comprehensive coverage

**Code Quality:** Good security practice

### config/initializers/assets.rb
- Standard assets configuration, version set to "1.0"

**Code Quality:** Fine

### config/environments/development.rb
- Proper development settings: reloading enabled, eager loading disabled
- Memory caching enabled
- Server timing enabled for performance debugging

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

### config/environments/test.rb
- Standard test environment configuration
- Eager loading only in CI environments (ENV["CI"])
- Forgery protection disabled for tests

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

### config/environments/production.rb
- **FINDING:** `config.force_ssl = true` enabled (good)
- **FINDING:** `config.cache_store = :memory_store` — this is problematic for multi-server deployments
- **Issue:** Comment suggests switching to Redis/Memcached, but no environment variable support
- **Recommendation:** 
  ```ruby
  config.cache_store = ENV["REDIS_URL"].present? ? :redis_cache_store : :memory_store
  ```
- Proper logging to STDOUT with request ID tagging
- Proper SSL/security configuration

**Code Quality:** Mostly good; cache store configuration needs attention for scaling

### config/importmap.rb
- Standard importmap configuration for Stimulus, Turbo, and controllers

**Code Quality:** Standard, fine

### config/puma.rb
- Standard Puma configuration
- Thread pool defaults to 3 (appropriate for Rails)
- Port defaulting to 3000

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

---

## 2. APP STRUCTURE

### app/controllers/application_controller.rb
- **FINDING:** Extremely minimal — only 4 lines
- Implements `stale_when_importmap_changes` for ETags
- No global error handling, authentication, or authorization

**Code Quality:** Minimal but appropriate for this stateless app

### app/helpers/application_helper.rb
- Empty helper file

**Code Quality:** Fine; no unused code

### app/helpers/forecasts_helper.rb
- Two helper methods:
  1. `gradient_class_for(weather_code)` — delegates to WeatherCode
  2. `format_temperature(temp)` — rounds temp, returns "--" for nil
- Both methods are defensive and well-documented

**Code Quality:** Good; proper nil handling

### app/models/current_weather.rb
- Value object (PORO) with 7 attributes as immutable readers
- Constructor validates all required parameters
- No business logic

**Code Quality:** Excellent; clean, simple, immutable

### app/models/day_forecast.rb
- Value object (PORO) with 6 attributes as immutable readers
- Constructor validates all required parameters
- No business logic

**Code Quality:** Excellent; clean, simple, immutable

### app/models/forecast_result.rb
- Value object wrapping complete forecast with cache metadata
- Default `daily_forecast` to empty array (defensive)
- Includes `cached?` predicate method
- Properly documented

**Code Quality:** Excellent; good defensive defaults

### app/models/weather_code.rb
- Maps WMO weather codes (0-99) to descriptions, emoji, and Tailwind gradient classes
- Uses frozen hash constants for performance
- Provides accessor methods: `for()`, `description()`, `emoji()`, `gradient()`
- Includes DEFAULT for unknown codes
- Well-documented with WMO reference

**Code Quality:** Excellent; comprehensive coverage of weather codes, defensive design

---

## 3. SERVICES

### app/services/weather_service.rb
- Fetches weather from Open-Meteo API (free, no key)
- **Architecture:** Factory pattern with `self.call()` class method
- Parses current conditions + 5-day forecast
- **FINDING:** Good error handling:
  - Validates response success
  - Checks for nil current weather
  - Includes `parse_date()` fallback for malformed dates
- Requests specific fields: temperature, humidity, wind, weather codes
- Converts to Fahrenheit and mph

**Code Quality:** Good; proper error handling, defensive parsing

**Potential Issues:**
- No retry logic for transient failures
- 10-second timeout may be tight under load
- No logging of API calls or errors

---

## 4. JAVASCRIPT

### app/javascript/application.js
- Standard entry point importing Turbo and controllers

**Code Quality:** Standard

### app/javascript/controllers/application.js
- Initializes Stimulus application with `debug = false`
- Exports application instance

**Code Quality:** Standard

### app/javascript/controllers/index.js
- Eager loads all controllers from the controllers directory

**Code Quality:** Standard

### app/javascript/controllers/unit_toggle_controller.js
- **ARCHITECTURE:** Stimulus controller for °F ↔ °C and mph ↔ km/h conversion
- Stores preference in localStorage
- **FINDING:** Reads data-temp-f and data-wind-mph attributes from HTML
- Converts temperatures: `(f - 32) * 5 / 9`
- Converts wind: `mph * 1.60934`

**Code Quality:** Good; localStorage persistence works correctly

**Potential Issues:**
- No error handling for parseFloat if data attributes are malformed
- Could add validation: `if (isNaN(f)) return` ✓ (already does this!)
- Toggle buttons use `classList.toggle()` which could be simplified with a calculated class

---

## 5. STYLESHEETS

### app/assets/stylesheets/application.css
- Standard manifest file
- Mostly empty comments

**Code Quality:** Fine

### app/assets/tailwind/application.css
- Imports Tailwind v4
- **FINDING:** `@source "../../models/*.rb"` — scans model files for dynamic classes
- Custom theme: system font stack with -apple-system, SF Pro Display, etc.

**Code Quality:** Excellent; proper Tailwind v4 setup with dynamic class scanning

---

## 6. CONFIGURATION FILES

### .rubocop.yml
- Inherits from `rubocop-rails-omakase`
- No custom rules overridden

**Code Quality:** Standard; uses Rails community style guide

### config/bundler-audit.yml
- **FINDING:** Empty ignore list with placeholder CVE-THAT-DOES-NOT-APPLY
- Could be removed or populated if real CVEs need ignoring

**Code Quality:** Fine; no vulnerabilities ignored

### Procfile.dev
- Two processes: web (Rails server) and css (Tailwind watcher)
- Standard Foreman configuration

**Code Quality:** Standard

### bin/dev
- Installs Foreman if missing
- Sets PORT to 3000 if unspecified
- Enables remote debugging with lazy loading
- Starts Foreman

**Code Quality:** Good; defensive, allows remote debugging

---

## 7. DOCUMENTATION

### README.md
- Comprehensive documentation
- Clear feature list with specific examples
- Architecture diagram showing data flow
- Full tech stack listed
- Setup instructions clear
- Project structure well-documented
- Caching behavior clearly explained
- Error handling documented
- Testing coverage mentioned (74 tests, 402 assertions)
- Deployment instructions provided

**Code Quality:** Excellent documentation; professional quality

---

## AUDIT FINDINGS

### CRITICAL ISSUES

1. **Production Cache Configuration** (`config/environments/production.rb`)
   - Using `:memory_store` for multi-server deployments will cause cache inconsistency
   - **Fix:** Add conditional Redis support:
   ```ruby
   config.cache_store = ENV["REDIS_URL"].present? ? :redis_cache_store : :memory_store
   ```

2. **Missing Content Security Policy** (`config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb`)
   - CSP is entirely disabled/commented out
   - **Risk:** XSS vulnerabilities possible
   - **Fix:** Enable CSP even in basic form

### HIGH PRIORITY ISSUES

3. **Weather API Error Handling** (`app/services/weather_service.rb`)
   - No retry logic for transient failures
   - 10-second timeout may be insufficient for slow API responses
   - No structured logging of API errors for monitoring

4. **JavaScript Unit Conversion** (`unit_toggle_controller.js`)
   - Wind conversion is not rounding: `(mph * 1.60934).toFixed(1)` uses toFixed but could be inconsistent with temp rounding
   - Should standardize rounding approach

### MEDIUM PRIORITY ISSUES

5. **Bundler Audit Configuration** (`config/bundler-audit.yml`)
   - Contains placeholder CVE comment (`CVE-THAT-DOES-NOT-APPLY`)
   - Should be removed if no CVEs are ignored

### CODE QUALITY OBSERVATIONS

**Strengths:**
- Clean, minimal architecture with service objects
- Excellent use of value objects (PORO pattern)
- Good defensive programming (nil checks, Date parsing fallbacks)
- Comprehensive weather code mapping
- Client-side unit toggle avoids server round-trips
- Proper use of Tailwind v4 with dynamic class scanning
- Great documentation

**Technical Debt & Improvements:**
- Add structured logging for API calls and errors
- Add retry logic to weather service with exponential backoff
- Consider adding monitoring/alerting for API failures
- Add unit tests for edge cases in unit_toggle_controller.js
- Extract magic constants (30-minute cache TTL, 5-day forecast) to configuration
- Consider circuit breaker pattern for external API calls
- Add metrics/instrumentation for cache hit rates

**Unused Code:**
- None identified; codebase is clean

**Bugs Found:**
- None critical identified; code is defensively written

---

## SUMMARY

This is a well-structured, clean Rails application. The architecture is appropriate for a stateless weather service. Main concerns are around production deployment (cache store configuration) and security (CSP). The codebase is well-documented, properly tested (per README), and uses good Ruby/Rails patterns throughout.

1	# This configuration file will be evaluated by Puma. The top-level methods that
2	# are invoked here are part of Puma's configuration DSL. For more information
3	# about methods provided by the DSL, see https://puma.io/puma/Puma/DSL.html.
4	#
5	# Puma starts a configurable number of processes (workers) and each process
6	# serves each request in a thread from an internal thread pool.
7	#
8	# You can control the number of workers using ENV["WEB_CONCURRENCY"]. You
9	# should only set this value when you want to run 2 or more workers. The
10	# default is already 1. You can set it to `auto` to automatically start a worker
11	# for each available processor.
12	#
13	# The ideal number of threads per worker depends both on how much time the
14	# application spends waiting for IO operations and on how much you wish to
15	# prioritize throughput over latency.
16	#
17	# As a rule of thumb, increasing the number of threads will increase how much
18	# traffic a given process can handle (throughput), but due to CRuby's
19	# Global VM Lock (GVL) it has diminishing returns and will degrade the
20	# response time (latency) of the application.
21	#
22	# The default is set to 3 threads as it's deemed a decent compromise between
23	# throughput and latency for the average Rails application.
24	#
25	# Any libraries that use a connection pool or another resource pool should
26	# be configured to provide at least as many connections as the number of
27	# threads. This includes Active Record's `pool` parameter in `database.yml`.
28	threads_count = ENV.fetch("RAILS_MAX_THREADS", 3)
29	threads threads_count, threads_count
30	
31	# Specifies the `port` that Puma will listen on to receive requests; default is 3000.
32	port ENV.fetch("PORT", 3000)
33	
34	# Allow puma to be restarted by `bin/rails restart` command.
35	plugin :tmp_restart
36	
37	# Specify the PID file. Defaults to tmp/pids/server.pid in development.
38	# In other environments, only set the PID file if requested.
39	pidfile ENV["PIDFILE"] if ENV["PIDFILE"]
40
```
</details></tool-use>

3	module ForecastsHelper
4	  # Returns the Tailwind gradient classes for a given WMO weather code.
5	  # Used to set the page background based on current conditions.
6	  def gradient_class_for(weather_code)

11	  # Returns "--" if the value is nil (defensive against missing API data).

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Value object wrapping a complete forecast response with cache metadata.
4	# Built by ForecastService to carry all data the view layer needs in one object.
5	class ForecastResult
6	  attr_reader :address, :zip_code, :current, :daily_forecast,
7	              :cached, :cached_at
8	
9	  def initialize(address:, zip_code:, current:, daily_forecast:, cached: false, cached_at: nil)
10	    @address = address
11	    @zip_code = zip_code
12	    @current = current
13	    @daily_forecast = daily_forecast || []
14	    @cached = cached
15	    @cached_at = cached_at
16	  end
17	
18	  # Whether this result was served from cache rather than a fresh API call.
19	  def cached?
20	    @cached
21	  end
22	end
23

1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Maps WMO (World Meteorological Organization) weather interpretation codes
4	# to human-readable descriptions, emoji icons, and Tailwind CSS gradient classes.
5	#
6	# Reference: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs#weathervariables
7	# WMO Code Table 4677 defines standard weather condition codes used by
8	# meteorological services worldwide.
9	class WeatherCode
10	  CODES = {
11	    0  => { description: "Clear Sky",        emoji: "☀️",   gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
12	    1  => { description: "Mainly Clear",     emoji: "🌤️",  gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
13	    2  => { description: "Partly Cloudy",    emoji: "⛅",    gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-slate-400" },
14	    3  => { description: "Overcast",         emoji: "☁️",   gradient: "from-slate-400 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
15	    45 => { description: "Fog",              emoji: "🌫️",  gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
16	    48 => { description: "Depositing Rime Fog", emoji: "🌫️", gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
17	    51 => { description: "Light Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
18	    53 => { description: "Moderate Drizzle", emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
19	    55 => { description: "Dense Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-slate-500 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
20	    61 => { description: "Slight Rain",      emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-600 via-slate-600 to-gray-600" },
21	    63 => { description: "Moderate Rain",    emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
22	    65 => { description: "Heavy Rain",       emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
23	    71 => { description: "Slight Snow",      emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-200 via-slate-300 to-gray-400" },
24	    73 => { description: "Moderate Snow",    emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
25	    75 => { description: "Heavy Snow",       emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
26	    77 => { description: "Snow Grains",      emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
27	    80 => { description: "Slight Showers",   emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-600" },
28	    81 => { description: "Moderate Showers", emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
29	    82 => { description: "Violent Showers",  emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
30	    85 => { description: "Slight Snow Showers", emoji: "🌨️", gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
31	    86 => { description: "Heavy Snow Showers",  emoji: "❄️",  gradient: "from-slate-400 via-gray-500 to-gray-600" },
32	    95 => { description: "Thunderstorm",     emoji: "⛈️",   gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
33	    96 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Slight Hail", emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
34	    99 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Heavy Hail",  emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-800 via-slate-900 to-gray-950" }
35	  }.each_value(&:freeze).freeze
36	
37	  DEFAULT = { description: "Unknown", emoji: "🌡️", gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-blue-600" }.freeze
38	
39	  # Returns the full info hash { description:, emoji:, gradient: } for a WMO code.
40	  def self.for(code)
41	    CODES.fetch(code, DEFAULT)
42	  end
43	
44	  def self.description(code)
45	    self.for(code)[:description]
46	  end
47	
48	  def self.emoji(code)
49	    self.for(code)[:emoji]
50	  end
51	
52	  def self.gradient(code)
53	    self.for(code)[:gradient]
54	  end
55	end
56

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/services/weather_service.rb`

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Fetches weather data from the Open-Meteo API (https://open-meteo.com).
4	# No API key required. Returns current conditions and a multi-day forecast.
5	#
6	# Open-Meteo uses WMO weather interpretation codes (mapped by WeatherCode).
7	# API docs: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs
8	class WeatherService
9	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
12	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
13	
14	  # Current weather fields requested from Open-Meteo
15	  CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
16	
17	  # Daily forecast fields requested from Open-Meteo
18	  DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
19	
20	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
21	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
22	  end
23	
24	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
25	    @latitude = latitude
26	    @longitude = longitude
27	  end
28	
29	  def call
30	    response = fetch_forecast
31	    validate_response!(response)
32	    parse_response(response)
33	  end
34	
35	  private
36	
37	  def fetch_forecast
38	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, {
39	      query: {
40	        latitude: @latitude,
41	        longitude: @longitude,
42	        current: CURRENT_PARAMS,
43	        daily: DAILY_PARAMS,
44	        temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
45	        wind_speed_unit: "mph",
46	        forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
47	        timezone: "auto"
48	      },
49	      timeout: 10 # seconds — Open-Meteo can be slow under load
50	    })
51	  end
52	
53	  def validate_response!(response)
54	    return if response.success?
55	
56	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Weather service error (HTTP #{response.code})"
57	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
58	  end
59	
60	  def parse_response(response)
61	    current = build_current_weather(response["current"])
62	    raise WeatherApiError, "Weather data unavailable — no current conditions returned" if current.nil?
63	
64	    {
65	      current: current,
66	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
67	    }
68	  end
69	
70	  def build_current_weather(data)
71	    return nil if data.nil?
72	
73	    # Fetch the full WMO code entry once to avoid repeated hash lookups
74	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
75	    weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
76	
77	    CurrentWeather.new(
78	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
79	      condition: weather_info[:description],
80	      icon: weather_info[:emoji],
81	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
82	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
83	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
84	      weather_code: code
85	    )
86	  end
87	
88	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
89	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
90	
91	    dates = data["time"]
92	    dates.each_index.map do |i|
93	      # Fetch the full WMO code entry once per day
94	      code = data.dig("weather_code", i).to_i
95	      weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
96	
97	      DayForecast.new(
98	        date: parse_date(dates[i]),
99	        high_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_max", i),
100	        low_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_min", i),
101	        condition: weather_info[:description],
102	        icon: weather_info[:emoji],
103	        chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0
104	      )
105	    end
106	  end
107	
108	  # Safely parse a date string, falling back to today if malformed.
109	  def parse_date(date_str)
110	    Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
111	  rescue Date::Error
112	    Date.current
113	  end
114	end
115
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./app/javascript/controllers/unit_toggle_controller.js`

```text
1	import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus"
2	
3	// Toggles all temperature displays between °F and °C, and wind between mph and km/h.
4	// Persists the user's preference in localStorage across sessions.
5	export default class extends Controller {
6	  static targets = ["temp", "wind", "windUnit", "btnF", "btnC"]
7	
8	  connect() {
9	    this.unit = localStorage.getItem("weatherUnit") || "F"
10	    this.apply()
11	  }
12	
13	  toggle(event) {
14	    this.unit = event.params.unit
15	    localStorage.setItem("weatherUnit", this.unit)
16	    this.apply()
17	  }
18	
19	  apply() {
20	    const isMetric = this.unit === "C"
21	
22	    this.tempTargets.forEach(el => {
23	      const f = parseFloat(el.dataset.tempF)
24	      if (isNaN(f)) return
25	      el.textContent = isMetric ? Math.round((f - 32) * 5 / 9) : Math.round(f)
26	    })
27	
28	    this.windTargets.forEach(el => {
29	      const mph = parseFloat(el.dataset.windMph)
30	      if (isNaN(mph)) return
31	      el.textContent = isMetric ? (mph * 1.60934).toFixed(1) : mph
32	    })
33	
34	    this.windUnitTargets.forEach(el => {
35	      el.textContent = isMetric ? "km/h" : "mph"
36	    })
37	
38	    if (this.hasBtnFTarget && this.hasBtnCTarget) {
39	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", !isMetric)
40	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", !isMetric)
41	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", isMetric)
42	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", isMetric)
43	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", isMetric)
44	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", isMetric)
45	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", !isMetric)
46	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", !isMetric)
47	    }
48	  }
49	}
50
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
1	# Weather Forecast
2	
3	A Ruby on Rails weather forecast application with an Apple Weather-inspired design. Enter any address, city, or zip/postal code to view current conditions and a 5-day extended forecast with real-time data.
4	
5	## Features
6	
7	- **Current conditions** -- temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed, and weather description with emoji icons
8	- **5-day extended forecast** -- daily high/low temperatures, weather conditions, and precipitation probability with labeled column headers
9	- **Unit toggle (°F / °C)** -- switch between Fahrenheit/mph and Celsius/km/h with a single click; preference persists across sessions via localStorage
10	- **Address autocomplete** -- type-ahead suggestions powered by Nominatim; supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Escape) and click selection
11	- **Canadian postal code support** -- handles A1A 1A1 format postal codes (with or without space, any case) by auto-appending country context for Nominatim
12	- **30-minute caching by postal code** -- subsequent requests for the same postal code are served from cache, with a visual indicator distinguishing fresh vs. cached results
13	- **Reverse-geocode fallback** -- city-level queries that lack a postal code automatically resolve via reverse geocoding
14	- **Apple Weather-inspired UI** -- glass morphism cards, weather-contextual gradient backgrounds, system font stack, responsive typography
15	- **Clear labels** -- "High" / "Low" labels, column headers on the 5-day forecast (Day, Rain, Condition, High, Low), condition descriptions visible alongside emoji icons, "Current Details" section header
16	- **Accessible** -- semantic HTML, ARIA labels, screen-reader-only labels, `role="status"` for cache indicators, `role="listbox"` for autocomplete
17	- **No API keys required** -- uses Open-Meteo (weather) and Nominatim/OpenStreetMap (geocoding), both free and keyless
18	- **No database** -- fully stateless, service-based architecture
19	
20	## Tech Stack
21	
22	| Component | Technology |
23	|-----------|-----------|
24	| Framework | Ruby 3.4.5, Rails 8.1 |
25	| Weather API | [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com) (free, no key) |
26	| Geocoding | [Nominatim](https://nominatim.org) via `geocoder` gem (free, no key) |
27	| HTTP Client | `httparty` |
28	| CSS | Tailwind CSS v4 via `tailwindcss-rails` (standalone binary, no Node.js) |
29	| Frontend | Hotwire (Turbo Drive), Stimulus |
30	| Asset Pipeline | Propshaft, importmap-rails |
31	| Server | Puma |
32	| Caching | `Rails.cache` with `:memory_store` (30-min TTL by postal code) |
33	
34	## Architecture
35	
36	```
37	User types in search field
38	  -> autocomplete_controller.js (Stimulus) debounces 300ms
39	    -> GET /autocomplete?q=... -> Nominatim suggestions -> dropdown
40	  -> User selects / submits form
41	
42	  -> ForecastsController#show
43	    -> ForecastService (orchestrator)
44	      -> GeocodingService (Nominatim) -> postal_code + lat/lon
45	         (Canadian postal codes auto-retry with ", Canada" suffix)
46	      -> Check Rails.cache["forecast_v1/{postal_code}"]
47	        -> HIT:  return cached data (cached=true, cached_at timestamp)
48	        -> MISS: WeatherService (Open-Meteo) -> cache 30 min -> return fresh
49	    -> Render show.html.erb with ForecastResult
50	
51	  -> unit_toggle_controller.js (Stimulus)
52	    -> Reads data-temp-f / data-wind-mph attributes
53	    -> Converts and updates all displayed values client-side
54	    -> Persists preference in localStorage
55	```
56	
57	### Key Design Decisions
58	
59	- **Cache key is postal code** -- all addresses within the same postal code share a single cache entry, reducing redundant API calls
60	- **`Rails.cache.read`/`write` instead of `.fetch`** -- allows distinguishing cache hits from misses so the view can show the correct indicator
61	- **Service object pattern** -- each service has a single responsibility: `GeocodingService` (address to coordinates), `WeatherService` (coordinates to weather), `ForecastService` (orchestration + caching)
62	- **PORO value objects** -- `ForecastResult`, `CurrentWeather`, `DayForecast`, `WeatherCode` are plain Ruby objects with no framework dependencies
63	- **Client-side unit toggle** -- temperatures stored as Fahrenheit in `data-temp-f` attributes; Stimulus converts on the fly without page reload
64	- **Server-side autocomplete proxy** -- `/autocomplete` proxies to Nominatim to maintain User-Agent header and avoid CORS issues
65	
66	## Getting Started
67	
68	### Prerequisites
69	
70	- Ruby 3.4.5 (see `.ruby-version`)
71	- Bundler
72	
73	### Setup
74	
75	```bash
76	git clone <repo-url> && cd forecastdemo
77	bin/setup
78	```
79	
80	This installs gems and prepares the environment. No database setup is needed.
81	
82	### Run the App
83	
84	```bash
85	bin/dev
86	```
87	
88	This starts both the Rails server and the Tailwind CSS watcher via Foreman. Visit [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).
89	
90	Alternatively, build CSS once and start the server directly:
91	
92	```bash
93	bin/rails tailwindcss:build
94	bin/rails server
95	```
96	
97	### Run Tests
98	
99	```bash
100	bin/rails test
101	```
102	
103	Runs the full test suite: 74 tests, 402 assertions. All tests use stubs for external API calls -- no network access required.
104	
105	### Linting and Security
106	
107	```bash
108	bin/rubocop          # Ruby style checks (RuboCop + Rails Omakase)
109	bin/brakeman         # Static security analysis
110	bin/bundler-audit    # Gem vulnerability audit
111	bin/ci               # Runs all of the above + tests
112	```
113	
114	## Project Structure
115	
116	```
117	app/
118	  models/
119	    weather_code.rb          # WMO code -> description, emoji, gradient mapping
120	    current_weather.rb       # Value object: current conditions
121	    day_forecast.rb          # Value object: single day forecast
122	    forecast_result.rb       # Value object: complete forecast + cache metadata
123	  services/
124	    geocoding_service.rb     # Address -> {postal_code, lat, lon} via Nominatim
125	    weather_service.rb       # Lat/lon -> weather data via Open-Meteo API
126	    forecast_service.rb      # Orchestrator: geocode -> cache -> fetch -> result
127	  controllers/
128	    forecasts_controller.rb  # index + show + autocomplete actions
129	  helpers/
130	    forecasts_helper.rb      # gradient_class_for(), format_temperature()
131	  javascript/controllers/
132	    unit_toggle_controller.js   # °F/°C toggle with localStorage persistence
133	    autocomplete_controller.js  # Debounced address suggestions with keyboard nav
134	  views/
135	    forecasts/
136	      index.html.erb         # Search screen with autocomplete
137	      show.html.erb          # Weather display with unit toggle + labels
138	    layouts/
139	      application.html.erb   # Base layout with Apple system font stack
140	  assets/
141	    tailwind/
142	      application.css        # Tailwind v4 config with @source for model scanning
143	
144	test/
145	  models/                    # WeatherCode mapping, ForecastResult defaults
146	  services/                  # Geocoding (incl. Canadian postal codes), Weather API, cache
147	  controllers/               # Routes, errors, network errors, XSS, autocomplete endpoint
148	  integration/               # Full user flows, unit toggle presence, autocomplete presence
149	
150	config/
151	  initializers/
152	    geocoder.rb              # Nominatim config with User-Agent and 1-day cache
153	  routes.rb                  # root + GET /forecast + GET /autocomplete
154	```
155	
156	## Caching Behavior
157	
158	| Cache Layer | TTL | Key | Purpose |
159	|-------------|-----|-----|---------|
160	| Forecast data | 30 minutes | `forecast_v1/{postal_code}` | Avoid redundant weather API calls |
161	| Geocoding results | 1 day | `geocoder:{address}` | Avoid redundant geocoding lookups |
162	
163	The cache indicator on the forecast page shows:
164	- **Green dot + "Just updated"** -- fresh data from the API
165	- **Amber dot + "Cached result from [time]"** -- served from cache
166	
167	For single-server deployments, the default `:memory_store` works well. For multi-server production, switch to Redis or Memcached in `config/environments/production.rb`:
168	
169	```ruby
170	config.cache_store = :redis_cache_store, { url: ENV["REDIS_URL"] }
171	```
172	
173	## Error Handling
174	
175	| Scenario | User Experience |
176	|----------|----------------|
177	| Blank/missing address | Redirect to home with "Please enter an address" |
178	| Address not found | Redirect with "Could not find location for '...'" |
179	| No postal code resolvable | Redirect with "Could not determine postal code... Try a more specific address" |
180	| Weather API error | Redirect with "Weather data unavailable: ..." |
181	| Network timeout / DNS failure | Redirect with "Unable to reach weather services. Please try again" |
182	
183	All errors are shown as styled flash alerts on the search screen. No raw error pages are exposed to users.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	The test suite covers:
188	
189	- **Unit tests** -- WeatherCode mappings, ForecastResult value object, nil defaults
190	- **Service tests** -- Geocoding (reverse-geocode fallback, Canadian postal codes with/without space, lowercase), Weather API parsing (WMO codes, nil data, unknown codes), ForecastService (cache hit/miss/expiry, zip-based sharing, error propagation)
191	- **Controller tests** -- All routes, error paths, network errors (timeout, DNS, connection refused), XSS prevention, autocomplete endpoint (valid query, short query, blank query, error handling, result limit), edge cases (whitespace, missing params)
192	- **Integration tests** -- Full user flows with labels, unit toggle data attributes, autocomplete presence on both pages, cache indicators, accessibility checks
193	
194	All external API calls are stubbed using a custom `StubHelper` module compatible with Minitest 6.
195	
196	## Deployment
197	
198	The app is ready for single-server deployment. Key production settings (`config/environments/production.rb`):
199	
200	- SSL forced (`config.force_ssl = true`)
201	- Eager loading enabled
202	- Memory cache store (swap to Redis for multi-server)
203	- Thruster for HTTP caching/compression (`bin/thrust`)
204	
205	```bash
206	RAILS_ENV=production bin/rails tailwindcss:build
207	RAILS_ENV=production bin/thrust bin/rails server
208	```
209	
210	## License
211	
212	This project is for demonstration purposes.
213
```
</details></tool-use>

1	#!/usr/bin/env sh
2	
3	if ! gem list foreman -i --silent; then
4	  echo "Installing foreman..."

6	fi
7

## AUDIT REPORT: Weather Forecast Rails App

## 1. CONFIG FILES

### config/application.rb
- Standard Rails 8.1 configuration
- Uses minimal railtie requires (no database, jobs, cable, mailer)
- Sets `config.load_defaults 8.1` correctly
- Ignores `lib/assets` and `lib/tasks` from autoloading

**Code Quality:** Clean, minimal, appropriate

### config/routes.rb
- Simple routing: health check (`/up`), root to `forecasts#index`, `forecast` show action, and autocomplete endpoint
- All routes clearly defined

**Code Quality:** Clean and maintainable

### config/initializers/geocoder.rb
- Nominatim geocoding configured with proper User-Agent
- 10-second timeout appropriate for external API
- Cache enabled with 1-day TTL with `geocoder:` prefix
- Uses `Rails.cache` correctly

**Code Quality:** Well-documented, secure (proper User-Agent handling)

### config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb
- **FINDING:** Entire CSP configuration is commented out
- **Risk:** No CSP protection in production
- **Recommendation:** Enable CSP for security. Should at least have:
  ```ruby
  config.content_security_policy do |policy|
    policy.default_src :self, :https
    policy.script_src :self, :https
    policy.style_src :self, :https
  end
  ```

### config/initializers/inflections.rb
- Completely commented default file
- No custom inflections needed

**Code Quality:** Fine for this app

### config/initializers/filter_parameter_logging.rb
- Properly filters sensitive parameters: `:passw`, `:email`, `:secret`, `:token`, `:_key`, `:crypt`, `:salt`, `:certificate`, `:otp`, `:ssn`, `:cvv`, `:cvc`
- Comprehensive coverage

**Code Quality:** Good security practice

### config/initializers/assets.rb
- Standard assets configuration, version set to "1.0"

**Code Quality:** Fine

### config/environments/development.rb
- Proper development settings: reloading enabled, eager loading disabled
- Memory caching enabled
- Server timing enabled for performance debugging

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

### config/environments/test.rb
- Standard test environment configuration
- Eager loading only in CI environments (ENV["CI"])
- Forgery protection disabled for tests

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

### config/environments/production.rb
- **FINDING:** `config.force_ssl = true` enabled (good)
- **FINDING:** `config.cache_store = :memory_store` — this is problematic for multi-server deployments
- **Issue:** Comment suggests switching to Redis/Memcached, but no environment variable support
- **Recommendation:**

  config.cache_store = ENV["REDIS_URL"].present? ? :redis_cache_store : :memory_store
  ```
- Proper logging to STDOUT with request ID tagging
- Proper SSL/security configuration

**Code Quality:** Mostly good; cache store configuration needs attention for scaling

### config/importmap.rb
- Standard importmap configuration for Stimulus, Turbo, and controllers

**Code Quality:** Standard, fine

### config/puma.rb
- Standard Puma configuration
- Thread pool defaults to 3 (appropriate for Rails)
- Port defaulting to 3000

**Code Quality:** Standard, good

---

## 2. APP STRUCTURE

### app/controllers/application_controller.rb
- **FINDING:** Extremely minimal — only 4 lines
- Implements `stale_when_importmap_changes` for ETags
- No global error handling, authentication, or authorization

**Code Quality:** Minimal but appropriate for this stateless app

- Empty helper file

**Code Quality:** Fine; no unused code

- Both methods are defensive and well-documented

**Code Quality:** Good; proper nil handling

### app/models/current_weather.rb
- Value object (PORO) with 7 attributes as immutable readers
- Constructor validates all required parameters
- No business logic

- Value object (PORO) with 6 attributes as immutable readers
- Constructor validates all required parameters
- No business logic

**Code Quality:** Excellent; clean, simple, immutable

- Value object wrapping complete forecast with cache metadata
- Default `daily_forecast` to empty array (defensive)

- Properly documented

**Code Quality:** Excellent; good defensive defaults

### app/models/weather_code.rb
- Maps WMO weather codes (0-99) to descriptions, emoji, and Tailwind gradient classes
- Uses frozen hash constants for performance
- Provides accessor methods: `for()`, `description()`, `emoji()`, `gradient()`
- Includes DEFAULT for unknown codes

**Code Quality:** Excellent; comprehensive coverage of weather codes, defensive design

---

## 3. SERVICES

### app/services/weather_service.rb
- Fetches weather from Open-Meteo API (free, no key)
- **Architecture:** Factory pattern with `self.call()` class method
- Parses current conditions + 5-day forecast
- **FINDING:** Good error handling:
  - Validates response success
  - Checks for nil current weather
  - Includes `parse_date()` fallback for malformed dates
- Requests specific fields: temperature, humidity, wind, weather codes
- Converts to Fahrenheit and mph

**Code Quality:** Good; proper error handling, defensive parsing

**Potential Issues:**
- No retry logic for transient failures
- 10-second timeout may be tight under load

**Code Quality:** Standard

- **ARCHITECTURE:** Stimulus controller for °F ↔ °C and mph ↔ km/h conversion
- Stores preference in localStorage
- **FINDING:** Reads data-temp-f and data-wind-mph attributes from HTML

---

## AUDIT FINDINGS

### CRITICAL ISSUES

1. **Production Cache Configuration** (`config/environments/production.rb`)
   - Using `:memory_store` for multi-server deployments will cause cache inconsistency
   - **Fix:** Add conditional Redis support:
   ```ruby
   config.cache_store = ENV["REDIS_URL"].present? ? :redis_cache_store : :memory_store
   ```

2. **Missing Content Security Policy** (`config/initializers/content_security_policy.rb`)
   - CSP is entirely disabled/commented out
   - **Risk:** XSS vulnerabilities possible
   - **Fix:** Enable CSP even in basic form

### HIGH PRIORITY ISSUES

3. **Weather API Error Handling** (`app/services/weather_service.rb`)
   - No retry logic for transient failures
   - 10-second timeout may be insufficient for slow API responses
   - No structured logging of API errors for monitoring

4. **JavaScript Unit Conversion** (`unit_toggle_controller.js`)
   - Wind conversion is not rounding: `(mph * 1.60934).toFixed(1)` uses toFixed but could be inconsistent with temp rounding
   - Should standardize rounding approach

### MEDIUM PRIORITY ISSUES

5. **Bundler Audit Configuration** (`config/bundler-audit.yml`)
   - Contains placeholder CVE comment (`CVE-THAT-DOES-NOT-APPLY`)
   - Should be removed if no CVEs are ignored

### CODE QUALITY OBSERVATIONS

**Strengths:**
- Clean, minimal architecture with service objects
- Excellent use of value objects (PORO pattern)
- Good defensive programming (nil checks, Date parsing fallbacks)
- Comprehensive weather code mapping
- Client-side unit toggle avoids server round-trips
- Proper use of Tailwind v4 with dynamic class scanning
- Great documentation

**Technical Debt & Improvements:**
- Add structured logging for API calls and errors
- Add retry logic to weather service with exponential backoff
- Consider adding monitoring/alerting for API failures
- Add unit tests for edge cases in unit_toggle_controller.js
- Extract magic constants (30-minute cache TTL, 5-day forecast) to configuration
- Consider circuit breaker pattern for external API calls
- Add metrics/instrumentation for cache hit rates

**Unused Code:**
- None identified; codebase is clean

**Bugs Found:**
- None critical identified; code is defensively written

---

## SUMMARY

This is a well-structured, clean Rails application. The architecture is appropriate for a stateless weather service. Main concerns are around production deployment (cache store configuration) and security (CSP). The codebase is well-documented, properly tested (per README), and uses good Ruby/Rails patterns throughout.

```text
1	# frozen_string_literal: true
2	
3	# Fetches weather data from the Open-Meteo API (https://open-meteo.com).
4	# No API key required. Returns current conditions and a multi-day forecast.
5	#
6	# Open-Meteo uses WMO weather interpretation codes (mapped by WeatherCode).
7	# API docs: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs
8	class WeatherService
9	  class WeatherApiError < StandardError; end
10	
11	  BASE_URL = "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast"
12	  FORECAST_DAYS = 5
13	
14	  # Current weather fields requested from Open-Meteo
15	  CURRENT_PARAMS = "temperature_2m,relative_humidity_2m,apparent_temperature,weather_code,wind_speed_10m"
16	
17	  # Daily forecast fields requested from Open-Meteo
18	  DAILY_PARAMS = "weather_code,temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,precipitation_probability_max"
19	
20	  def self.call(latitude:, longitude:)
21	    new(latitude: latitude, longitude: longitude).call
22	  end
23	
24	  def initialize(latitude:, longitude:)
25	    @latitude = latitude
26	    @longitude = longitude
27	  end
28	
29	  def call
30	    response = fetch_forecast
31	    validate_response!(response)
32	    parse_response(response)
33	  end
34	
35	  private
36	
37	  def fetch_forecast
38	    HTTParty.get(BASE_URL, {
39	      query: {
40	        latitude: @latitude,
41	        longitude: @longitude,
42	        current: CURRENT_PARAMS,
43	        daily: DAILY_PARAMS,
44	        temperature_unit: "fahrenheit",
45	        wind_speed_unit: "mph",
46	        forecast_days: FORECAST_DAYS,
47	        timezone: "auto"
48	      },
49	      timeout: 10 # seconds — Open-Meteo can be slow under load
50	    })
51	  end
52	
53	  def validate_response!(response)
54	    return if response.success?
55	
56	    error_message = response.dig("reason") || "Weather service error (HTTP #{response.code})"
57	    raise WeatherApiError, error_message
58	  end
59	
60	  def parse_response(response)
61	    current = build_current_weather(response["current"])
62	    raise WeatherApiError, "Weather data unavailable — no current conditions returned" if current.nil?
63	
64	    {
65	      current: current,
66	      daily_forecast: build_daily_forecast(response["daily"])
67	    }
68	  end
69	
70	  def build_current_weather(data)
71	    return nil if data.nil?
72	
73	    # Fetch the full WMO code entry once to avoid repeated hash lookups
74	    code = data["weather_code"].to_i
75	    weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
76	
77	    CurrentWeather.new(
78	      temperature_f: data["temperature_2m"],
79	      condition: weather_info[:description],
80	      icon: weather_info[:emoji],
81	      humidity: data["relative_humidity_2m"],
82	      wind_mph: data["wind_speed_10m"],
83	      feels_like_f: data["apparent_temperature"],
84	      weather_code: code
85	    )
86	  end
87	
88	  def build_daily_forecast(data)
89	    return [] if data.nil? || data["time"].nil?
90	
91	    dates = data["time"]
92	    dates.each_index.map do |i|
93	      # Fetch the full WMO code entry once per day
94	      code = data.dig("weather_code", i).to_i
95	      weather_info = WeatherCode.for(code)
96	
97	      DayForecast.new(
98	        date: parse_date(dates[i]),
99	        high_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_max", i),
100	        low_f: data.dig("temperature_2m_min", i),
101	        condition: weather_info[:description],
102	        icon: weather_info[:emoji],
103	        chance_of_rain: data.dig("precipitation_probability_max", i) || 0
104	      )
105	    end
106	  end
107	
108	  # Safely parse a date string, falling back to today if malformed.
109	  def parse_date(date_str)
110	    Date.parse(date_str.to_s)
111	  rescue Date::Error
112	    Date.current
113	  end
114	end
115
```
</details></tool-use>

3	# Maps WMO (World Meteorological Organization) weather interpretation codes

5	#
6	# Reference: https://open-meteo.com/en/docs#weathervariables
7	# WMO Code Table 4677 defines standard weather condition codes used by
8	# meteorological services worldwide.
9	class WeatherCode
10	  CODES = {
11	    0  => { description: "Clear Sky",        emoji: "☀️",   gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },
12	    1  => { description: "Mainly Clear",     emoji: "🌤️",  gradient: "from-sky-400 via-blue-400 to-blue-500" },

15	    45 => { description: "Fog",              emoji: "🌫️",  gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
16	    48 => { description: "Depositing Rime Fog", emoji: "🌫️", gradient: "from-gray-400 via-gray-500 to-slate-500" },
17	    51 => { description: "Light Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
18	    53 => { description: "Moderate Drizzle", emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-500" },
19	    55 => { description: "Dense Drizzle",    emoji: "🌦️",  gradient: "from-slate-500 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },

21	    63 => { description: "Moderate Rain",    emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },

23	    71 => { description: "Slight Snow",      emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-200 via-slate-300 to-gray-400" },
24	    73 => { description: "Moderate Snow",    emoji: "🌨️",  gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
25	    75 => { description: "Heavy Snow",       emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
26	    77 => { description: "Snow Grains",      emoji: "❄️",   gradient: "from-slate-300 via-gray-400 to-gray-500" },
27	    80 => { description: "Slight Showers",   emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-blue-500 via-slate-500 to-gray-600" },
28	    81 => { description: "Moderate Showers", emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-600 via-gray-600 to-gray-700" },
29	    82 => { description: "Violent Showers",  emoji: "🌧️",  gradient: "from-slate-700 via-gray-700 to-gray-800" },
30	    85 => { description: "Slight Snow Showers", emoji: "🌨️", gradient: "from-blue-300 via-slate-400 to-gray-500" },
31	    86 => { description: "Heavy Snow Showers",  emoji: "❄️",  gradient: "from-slate-400 via-gray-500 to-gray-600" },
32	    95 => { description: "Thunderstorm",     emoji: "⛈️",   gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
33	    96 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Slight Hail", emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-700 via-slate-800 to-gray-900" },
34	    99 => { description: "Thunderstorm with Heavy Hail",  emoji: "⛈️", gradient: "from-gray-800 via-slate-900 to-gray-950" }
35	  }.each_value(&:freeze).freeze
36	
37	  DEFAULT = { description: "Unknown", emoji: "🌡️", gradient: "from-blue-400 via-blue-500 to-blue-600" }.freeze
38	
39	  # Returns the full info hash { description:, emoji:, gradient: } for a WMO code.
40	  def self.for(code)
41	    CODES.fetch(code, DEFAULT)
42	  end
43	
44	  def self.description(code)
45	    self.for(code)[:description]
46	  end
47	
48	  def self.emoji(code)
49	    self.for(code)[:emoji]
50	  end
51	
52	  def self.gradient(code)
53	    self.for(code)[:gradient]
54	  end
55	end
56

1	# frozen_string_literal: true

3	# Configure the geocoder gem to use Nominatim (OpenStreetMap) for address lookup.
4	# Nominatim is free and requires no API key, but requires a descriptive User-Agent.
5	# See: https://nominatim.org/release-docs/develop/api/Search/
6	Geocoder.configure(
7	  lookup: :nominatim,
8	  language: :en,
9	  use_https: true,
10	  http_headers: {
11	    "User-Agent" => "ForecastDemo/1.0 (Rails weather forecast demo app)"
12	  },
13	  timeout: 10, # seconds — Nominatim can be slow under heavy load
14	  cache: Rails.cache,
15	  cache_options: {
16	    expiration: 1.day, # Geocoding results rarely change; cache aggressively
17	    prefix: "geocoder:"
18	  }
19	)
20
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/javascript/controllers/unit_toggle_controller.js`

```text
1	import { Controller } from "@hotwired/stimulus"
2	
3	// Toggles all temperature displays between °F and °C, and wind between mph and km/h.
4	// Persists the user's preference in localStorage across sessions.
5	export default class extends Controller {
6	  static targets = ["temp", "wind", "windUnit", "btnF", "btnC"]
7	
8	  connect() {
9	    this.unit = localStorage.getItem("weatherUnit") || "F"
10	    this.apply()
11	  }
12	
13	  toggle(event) {
14	    this.unit = event.params.unit
15	    localStorage.setItem("weatherUnit", this.unit)
16	    this.apply()
17	  }
18	
19	  apply() {
20	    const isMetric = this.unit === "C"
21	
22	    this.tempTargets.forEach(el => {
23	      const f = parseFloat(el.dataset.tempF)
24	      if (isNaN(f)) return
25	      el.textContent = isMetric ? Math.round((f - 32) * 5 / 9) : Math.round(f)
26	    })
27	
28	    this.windTargets.forEach(el => {
29	      const mph = parseFloat(el.dataset.windMph)
30	      if (isNaN(mph)) return
31	      el.textContent = isMetric ? (mph * 1.60934).toFixed(1) : mph
32	    })
33	
34	    this.windUnitTargets.forEach(el => {
35	      el.textContent = isMetric ? "km/h" : "mph"
36	    })
37	
38	    if (this.hasBtnFTarget && this.hasBtnCTarget) {
39	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", !isMetric)
40	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", !isMetric)
41	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", isMetric)
42	      this.btnFTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", isMetric)
43	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-white/25", isMetric)
44	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white", isMetric)
45	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("bg-transparent", !isMetric)
46	      this.btnCTarget.classList.toggle("text-white/50", !isMetric)
47	    }
48	  }
49	}
50
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
1	# Weather Forecast
2	
3	A Ruby on Rails weather forecast application with an Apple Weather-inspired design. Enter any address, city, or zip/postal code to view current conditions and a 5-day extended forecast with real-time data.
4	
5	## Features
6	
7	- **Current conditions** -- temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed, and weather description with emoji icons
8	- **5-day extended forecast** -- daily high/low temperatures, weather conditions, and precipitation probability with labeled column headers
9	- **Unit toggle (°F / °C)** -- switch between Fahrenheit/mph and Celsius/km/h with a single click; preference persists across sessions via localStorage
10	- **Address autocomplete** -- type-ahead suggestions powered by Nominatim; supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Escape) and click selection
11	- **Canadian postal code support** -- handles A1A 1A1 format postal codes (with or without space, any case) by auto-appending country context for Nominatim
12	- **30-minute caching by postal code** -- subsequent requests for the same postal code are served from cache, with a visual indicator distinguishing fresh vs. cached results
13	- **Reverse-geocode fallback** -- city-level queries that lack a postal code automatically resolve via reverse geocoding
14	- **Apple Weather-inspired UI** -- glass morphism cards, weather-contextual gradient backgrounds, system font stack, responsive typography
15	- **Clear labels** -- "High" / "Low" labels, column headers on the 5-day forecast (Day, Rain, Condition, High, Low), condition descriptions visible alongside emoji icons, "Current Details" section header
16	- **Accessible** -- semantic HTML, ARIA labels, screen-reader-only labels, `role="status"` for cache indicators, `role="listbox"` for autocomplete
17	- **No API keys required** -- uses Open-Meteo (weather) and Nominatim/OpenStreetMap (geocoding), both free and keyless
18	- **No database** -- fully stateless, service-based architecture
19	
20	## Tech Stack
21	
22	| Component | Technology |
23	|-----------|-----------|
24	| Framework | Ruby 3.4.5, Rails 8.1 |
25	| Weather API | [Open-Meteo](https://open-meteo.com) (free, no key) |
26	| Geocoding | [Nominatim](https://nominatim.org) via `geocoder` gem (free, no key) |
27	| HTTP Client | `httparty` |
28	| CSS | Tailwind CSS v4 via `tailwindcss-rails` (standalone binary, no Node.js) |
29	| Frontend | Hotwire (Turbo Drive), Stimulus |
30	| Asset Pipeline | Propshaft, importmap-rails |
31	| Server | Puma |
32	| Caching | `Rails.cache` with `:memory_store` (30-min TTL by postal code) |
33	
34	## Architecture
35	
36	```
37	User types in search field
38	  -> autocomplete_controller.js (Stimulus) debounces 300ms
39	    -> GET /autocomplete?q=... -> Nominatim suggestions -> dropdown
40	  -> User selects / submits form
41	
42	  -> ForecastsController#show
43	    -> ForecastService (orchestrator)
44	      -> GeocodingService (Nominatim) -> postal_code + lat/lon
45	         (Canadian postal codes auto-retry with ", Canada" suffix)
46	      -> Check Rails.cache["forecast_v1/{postal_code}"]
47	        -> HIT:  return cached data (cached=true, cached_at timestamp)
48	        -> MISS: WeatherService (Open-Meteo) -> cache 30 min -> return fresh
49	    -> Render show.html.erb with ForecastResult
50	
51	  -> unit_toggle_controller.js (Stimulus)
52	    -> Reads data-temp-f / data-wind-mph attributes
53	    -> Converts and updates all displayed values client-side
54	    -> Persists preference in localStorage
55	```
56	
57	### Key Design Decisions
58	
59	- **Cache key is postal code** -- all addresses within the same postal code share a single cache entry, reducing redundant API calls
60	- **`Rails.cache.read`/`write` instead of `.fetch`** -- allows distinguishing cache hits from misses so the view can show the correct indicator
61	- **Service object pattern** -- each service has a single responsibility: `GeocodingService` (address to coordinates), `WeatherService` (coordinates to weather), `ForecastService` (orchestration + caching)
62	- **PORO value objects** -- `ForecastResult`, `CurrentWeather`, `DayForecast`, `WeatherCode` are plain Ruby objects with no framework dependencies
63	- **Client-side unit toggle** -- temperatures stored as Fahrenheit in `data-temp-f` attributes; Stimulus converts on the fly without page reload
64	- **Server-side autocomplete proxy** -- `/autocomplete` proxies to Nominatim to maintain User-Agent header and avoid CORS issues
65	
66	## Getting Started
67	
68	### Prerequisites
69	
70	- Ruby 3.4.5 (see `.ruby-version`)
71	- Bundler
72	
73	### Setup
74	
75	```bash
76	git clone <repo-url> && cd forecastdemo
77	bin/setup
78	```
79	
80	This installs gems and prepares the environment. No database setup is needed.
81	
82	### Run the App
83	
84	```bash
85	bin/dev
86	```
87	
88	This starts both the Rails server and the Tailwind CSS watcher via Foreman. Visit [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000).
89	
90	Alternatively, build CSS once and start the server directly:
91	
92	```bash
93	bin/rails tailwindcss:build
94	bin/rails server
95	```
96	
97	### Run Tests
98	
99	```bash
100	bin/rails test
101	```
102	
103	Runs the full test suite: 74 tests, 402 assertions. All tests use stubs for external API calls -- no network access required.
104	
105	### Linting and Security
106	
107	```bash
108	bin/rubocop          # Ruby style checks (RuboCop + Rails Omakase)
109	bin/brakeman         # Static security analysis
110	bin/bundler-audit    # Gem vulnerability audit
111	bin/ci               # Runs all of the above + tests
112	```
113	
114	## Project Structure
115	
116	```
117	app/
118	  models/
119	    weather_code.rb          # WMO code -> description, emoji, gradient mapping
120	    current_weather.rb       # Value object: current conditions
121	    day_forecast.rb          # Value object: single day forecast
122	    forecast_result.rb       # Value object: complete forecast + cache metadata
123	  services/
124	    geocoding_service.rb     # Address -> {postal_code, lat, lon} via Nominatim
125	    weather_service.rb       # Lat/lon -> weather data via Open-Meteo API
126	    forecast_service.rb      # Orchestrator: geocode -> cache -> fetch -> result
127	  controllers/
128	    forecasts_controller.rb  # index + show + autocomplete actions
129	  helpers/
130	    forecasts_helper.rb      # gradient_class_for(), format_temperature()
131	  javascript/controllers/
132	    unit_toggle_controller.js   # °F/°C toggle with localStorage persistence
133	    autocomplete_controller.js  # Debounced address suggestions with keyboard nav
134	  views/
135	    forecasts/
136	      index.html.erb         # Search screen with autocomplete
137	      show.html.erb          # Weather display with unit toggle + labels
138	    layouts/
139	      application.html.erb   # Base layout with Apple system font stack
140	  assets/
141	    tailwind/
142	      application.css        # Tailwind v4 config with @source for model scanning
143	
144	test/
145	  models/                    # WeatherCode mapping, ForecastResult defaults
146	  services/                  # Geocoding (incl. Canadian postal codes), Weather API, cache
147	  controllers/               # Routes, errors, network errors, XSS, autocomplete endpoint
148	  integration/               # Full user flows, unit toggle presence, autocomplete presence
149	
150	config/
151	  initializers/
152	    geocoder.rb              # Nominatim config with User-Agent and 1-day cache
153	  routes.rb                  # root + GET /forecast + GET /autocomplete
154	```
155	
156	## Caching Behavior
157	
158	| Cache Layer | TTL | Key | Purpose |
159	|-------------|-----|-----|---------|
160	| Forecast data | 30 minutes | `forecast_v1/{postal_code}` | Avoid redundant weather API calls |
161	| Geocoding results | 1 day | `geocoder:{address}` | Avoid redundant geocoding lookups |
162	
163	The cache indicator on the forecast page shows:
164	- **Green dot + "Just updated"** -- fresh data from the API
165	- **Amber dot + "Cached result from [time]"** -- served from cache
166	
167	For single-server deployments, the default `:memory_store` works well. For multi-server production, switch to Redis or Memcached in `config/environments/production.rb`:
168	
169	```ruby
170	config.cache_store = :redis_cache_store, { url: ENV["REDIS_URL"] }
171	```
172	
173	## Error Handling
174	
175	| Scenario | User Experience |
176	|----------|----------------|
177	| Blank/missing address | Redirect to home with "Please enter an address" |
178	| Address not found | Redirect with "Could not find location for '...'" |
179	| No postal code resolvable | Redirect with "Could not determine postal code... Try a more specific address" |
180	| Weather API error | Redirect with "Weather data unavailable: ..." |
181	| Network timeout / DNS failure | Redirect with "Unable to reach weather services. Please try again" |
182	
183	All errors are shown as styled flash alerts on the search screen. No raw error pages are exposed to users.
184	
185	## Testing
186	
187	The test suite covers:
188	
189	- **Unit tests** -- WeatherCode mappings, ForecastResult value object, nil defaults
190	- **Service tests** -- Geocoding (reverse-geocode fallback, Canadian postal codes with/without space, lowercase), Weather API parsing (WMO codes, nil data, unknown codes), ForecastService (cache hit/miss/expiry, zip-based sharing, error propagation)
191	- **Controller tests** -- All routes, error paths, network errors (timeout, DNS, connection refused), XSS prevention, autocomplete endpoint (valid query, short query, blank query, error handling, result limit), edge cases (whitespace, missing params)
192	- **Integration tests** -- Full user flows with labels, unit toggle data attributes, autocomplete presence on both pages, cache indicators, accessibility checks
193	
194	All external API calls are stubbed using a custom `StubHelper` module compatible with Minitest 6.
195	
196	## Deployment
197	
198	The app is ready for single-server deployment. Key production settings (`config/environments/production.rb`):
199	
200	- SSL forced (`config.force_ssl = true`)
201	- Eager loading enabled
202	- Memory cache store (swap to Redis for multi-server)
203	- Thruster for HTTP caching/compression (`bin/thrust`)
204	
205	```bash
206	RAILS_ENV=production bin/rails tailwindcss:build
207	RAILS_ENV=production bin/thrust bin/rails server
208	```
209	
210	## License
211	
212	This project is for demonstration purposes.
213
```
</details></tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
OK, so the Tailwind v4 CSS only has `@source "../../models/*.rb"` for scanning model files. But by default, Tailwind v4 with `tailwindcss-rails` scans views, helpers, and JavaScript controllers automatically. Let me verify this is actually working by checking if the gray-800/gray-900 classes are compiled.

Actually, in Tailwind v4 with the Rails standalone binary, the default content paths are configured by the binary itself. The `tailwindcss-rails` gem's `tailwindcss:build` command passes content paths including `app/views/**/*`, `app/helpers/**/*`, `app/javascript/**/*`, and `app/assets/stylesheets/**/*`. 

The `@source` directive in the CSS file just adds additional paths on top of those defaults. Since the gem already handles scanning the standard Rails directories, I don't need to verify the gray-800 class compilation — the setup is correct. Now I can move forward with the cleanup tasks: removing `jbuilder` from the Gemfile and updating the Tailwind CSS file.
